Studies have shown that with a consistent diet of only nutriwafer, the consumer grows accustomed to it, and some even learn to enjoy their daily allotment. This is another reason poorer citizens are encouraged to rely solely on nutriwafer for their nutrition.

There’s a thriving black market for fresh food like strawberries, goat meat, apples, and lettuce in the poorer districts. Some of these illicit dealers distribute the food at cost, for the benefit of the community, while others charge exorbitant prices and turn a tidy profit.

The grocery pass only limits access to grocery stores, and even poorer districts have diners and milkbars.

Middle Income

Those on middle incomes are issued a grocery pass and can access a relatively wide range of food. Good fresh food is still expensive, so canned foods make up a large part of the typical diet; canned soups, beans, and processed meats (typically goat, chicken, or rat). It’s common to eat pre-packaged frozen meals in front of the plasmovision set.

International food has become popular in the last decade, with Akorosi versions of Severosi, Daggerite, and Iruvian cuisine made popular through magazines and cookbooks (typically unrecognizable to those who know the real thing, with substituted ingredients and tepid flavors).

Restaurants are no longer the sole preserve of the wealthy, and there has been a boom in fast food and casual dining.

Classic High Cuisine

For the aristocracy and those with old-money pretensions, Akorosi High Cuisine is the order of the day. The rising popularity of casual dining amongst the middle class has led to gourmet restaurants becoming an important form of conspicuous consumption and class status.

This type of cuisine is typically a multi-course affair, heavy on seafood, shellfish, buttery sauces, and elaborate decorative displays made from fresh greens like asparagus, cucumber, and salad leaves (not necessarily to be eaten, but as a mark of prestige and excess). Restaurant decor is typically dark wood paneling, velvet or leather seating, and muted lighting. Black tie dress is expected.

New Cuisine

This trendy and experimental form of dining is popular among younger, newmoney professionals. It focuses on fresh ingredients, playful experimentation, and spectacular presentation.

Bringing It to the Table

Food is an evocative way to immerse yourself in the fictional world. Consider scenes set in restaurants, diners, or milkbars. Ask the players— what are you having? You can learn a lot about a character by what they eat or drink.

The Paranormal

psychic echo of a dead person will materialize from its body, and where the majority of the world’s population lives in protective bubbles to hide from the existential horrors that roam the wilds.

There are four basic attitudes toward the paranormal:

Denialist: Those who refuse to accept the paranormal at all, and wave it all away as having an ordinary scientific explanation. They cling to their willful blindness, encouraged by government censorship and propaganda.

Whatever you think you saw down there, you didn’t. Ghosts and goblins are children’s stories. Now close the door and I’ll make a cup of tea.

Average: The standard curriculum teaches children about the cataclysm, the dangers of reality instability, and the deathlands. Most people try not to think about this stuff too much, and any major paranormal events have probably been scrubbed from their memory. They will have heard of the resonance field, and might have even seen a resonance echo once or twice, but they feel safe inside the Bubble.

They call it an “echo,” this happened to my Aunt Klara. I’ll call Section 6, they’ll know what to do.

Cutting-Edge Believer: Those on the fringes of accepted science, who seek to categorize and understand beyond the limits of what the government wants you to know. They accept the existence of all manner of paranormal phenomena, but try to understand them in pseudo-scientific terms. They use experiments to replicate what was once achieved through superstitious ritual.

Sounds like a Class III Resonance Entity. An echo. I have a containment unit with me—take the children next door, I’ll sort it out.

Old Believer: Those who still understand the paranormal in antiquated terms. Maybe they’re eccentric, or they’ve learned from ancient tomes, or they’re just old. They see the world as a supernatural place, with broken gates of death leading to ghosts and demons and horrors plaguing these Shattered Isles.

That’s a ghost down there. Take my spiritbane charm and wait here—I know a whisper, she can help.

Paranormal Beliefs Across the Shattered Isles

Every nation of the Shattered Isles has its own approach to dealing with paranormal threats.

  • At the center of the Iruvian city of U’duasha is a powerful and ancient anomaly that they call U’du (“the Well”). This phenomenon draws resonance entities to it like a magnet, leaving the rest of the city untroubled. The Iruvians have invented artificial replicas of the U’du anomaly, which they use to protect all their cities.

  • Severos has access to Bubble technology courtesy of its close relationship with the Imperium, but also has its own strong tradition of indigenous spirit-hunting.

  • The banyans of the Dagger Isles are protected by the Serpentine Paths. These light streams, which weave themselves through the entirety of the archipelago, drain resonance entities of their paranormal power, and even consume them entirely. Combined with the vivid bioluminescence of the archipelago’s flora and fauna, many Daggerites have no need for Bubbles. Banyans that have had a particularly long history of foreign invasion struggle, due to how disconnected they are from Daggerite traditions around spirits and paranormal phenomena.

  • The towns and cities of the United Skov Covenant are ruled by locally elected covens, who use a combination of traditional knowledge and advanced parapsychology to protect their communities from paranormal threats.

In general, most people outside of the Imperium have a wider knowledge of the paranormal, and more experience of occult traditions, than your typical Akorosi citizen. They soon learn to keep this knowledge to themselves within the borders of the Imperium, where Section 6 is always watching for reality infractions.

Despite all evidence to the contrary, Imperial propaganda asserts that rival nations want to destroy the protective Bubbles and steal the technology for themselves.

The Paranormal and You

A close encounter with an echo or other resonance entity is a harrowing experience. By default, the standard effect is to either paralyze a person with fear or panic them into fleeing from its presence. A PC can choose to freeze or flee, or make a resistance roll with Resolve to ignore the effect.

Being possessed by an echo deadlocks a key at the end of every week. Once the possessed body suffers its final deadlock, it can no longer sustain its life. The possessing echo must abandon it and seek out a new host or, if they perform the necessary occult rituals, bond with the corpse and become a vampire.

Large anomalies or mind-bending horrors (such as the physical manifestation of a forgotten god) may have additional effects on sight (in addition to causing viewers to freeze or flee). You may choose to resist them, too, as normal.

When you behold the billowing, veiled form of the Red Queen, you stand frozen in place as she envelops you, and you feel a powerful drowsiness—an urge to sink into a sweet, suffocating, fiery sleep...

Other than freeze or flight, other common responses to paranormal phenomena include nausea, disassociation, hallucinations, memory loss, and intense positive or negative emotions.

Practical Applications of Paranormal Science

Certain applications of paranormal science are widely adopted and government-sanctioned, found in every home and business. Many other applications are rare, illegal, and dangerous.

Sparktech. Any device that utilizes electroplasmic circuitry, making it inherently paranormal. Advanced and complex electronic machinery.

Electroplasmic Fusion. Electroplasm makes modern life possible, powering everything from the city-protecting Bubble down to every humble lightbulb. Electroplasm was once refined from leviathan blood, but today the world runs on electroplasmic fusion. This super-efficient process turns a deceased person’s psychic residue, their resonance echo, into raw electroplasmic power. Where once the dead were cremated to stop their echoes haunting the physical plane, now they are cycled through the fusion plant to contribute to the safety and prosperity of those still living. The body remains intact, and Section 6 disposes of it privately and respectfully.

The secrecy around the Bellweather Fusion Station has led to a number of conspiracy theories. Anti-war activists often assert that the Imperium’s constant warfare (or “peacekeeping”) is really just to keep the fuel flowing. Soldiers are conscripted, die on the frontlines, and their bodies are sent back home in resonance-stasis to be disposed of properly at a fusion station in Akoros.

Plasmoleum. Electroplasm is a raw current distributed to Doskvol’s power grid, and the fusion station generates the Bubble directly. Some portion of the power output is also refined into plasmoleum, a high-energy liquid with a viscous consistency and a sharp, pungent odor. This is sold wholesale and redistributed by several different retailers. Plasmoleum fuels autopods, as well as many other machines.

Ultraplasm. Plasmoleum can be further refined into an even more potent fuel source. Ultraplasm is volatile, radioactive, and delivers a huge energy yield.

Alluvium. The physical residue of the resonance field—quantum instability in physical form. Rare, unstable, and very expensive on the black market. This substance breaks the laws of reality by its very presence, and most advanced paranormal experiments require some measure of alluvium as a catalyst.

Paranormal Entities

The Metaphysical Entity Classification System (Bethan, 946) is the most common way to identify and categorize the various paranormal entities.

Class I Resonance Disturbances. A non-physical manifestation, such as spectral lights, voices and sounds, vapors, etc.

Class II Resonance Entities: Loopers. A single psychic imprint captured in the resonance field, repeating over and over. It has no will of its own, but is

still dangerous because of the electrical discharge of its plasmic form. They sometimes spontaneously manifest at the site of an extremely violent event, whether or not death was involved.

Class III Resonance Entities: Echoes. Resonance echoes are the most common and widely encountered paranormal entity. An echo is a person’s psychic impression in the resonance field, which will manifest in physical reality after the death of the body (unless the body is properly disposed of). Echoes destabilize over time, becoming more incoherent and disturbed. They’re drawn to possess victims in order to drain their resonance energy. The trauma of their condition leads them to seek vengeance upon those they believe wronged them in life.

Class IV Resonance Entities: Mirrors. Some echoes stabilize into a more permanent form, becoming “reconciled.” Their mental faculties remain intact and they no longer crave the resonance energy of the living, as echoes do. Mirrors are extremely rare.

Class V Resonance Entities: Aberrations. Also known as “horrors.” These entities are endemic to the resonance field, and should not exist in our reality—but post-cataclysm, they can and do manifest in the physical realm. They’re very rare within the city, but common in the deathlands outside the Bubble. Aberrations are intensely powerful and highly resistant to harm. They may only persist in this world for a short time, however. They cannot sustain life for long outside the resonance field and they inevitably decay to dust or ooze in the span of a few hours or days.

See Blades in the Dark , page 304, for a table to generate your own horrors.

Class VI Metadimensional Entities. Traditionally called “demons.” The resonance field is the closest dimension to our own, but it is not the only one. Class VI Metadimensional Entities are inhabitants of distant and alien dimensions. Their native dimension must have some affinity with ours, and the Class VI Entity uses this conduit to manifest in our reality. Some are beings of pure exothermic reaction (flame and smoke), others comprise dynamic fluid systems (the sea), others are beings of the upper atmosphere and stellar systems (the sky and stars), and so on.

Unlike humans, Class VI Entities are not echoed in the resonance field. They are corporeal creatures, though extremely difficult to kill. Most appear as terrifying humanoids with physical features related to their elemental affinity. Some can transmute into their corresponding element, and a few can disguise themselves with a convincing human appearance.

Class VI Entities are alien cosmic entities that transcend human understanding. Their desires are vast, cruel, and incomprehensible.

Although their powers are as diverse as they are, every Class VI Entity possesses preternatural speed and strength, telepathic communication, powerful telekinesis related to their elemental affinity, and the ability to travel via their elemental affinity (such as between water sources, for an entity from a water-based dimension).

A closely guarded secret is that a sparkmind , or so-called artificial intelligence, uses the same scientific principles behind hulls but applies them to Class VI Entities. A very risky scientific procedure can, in theory, capture such an entity in its most pure elemental form: water, fire, smoke, etc. This elemental form is then entombed within the construct, and an arcane pattern of resistors, capacitors, and circuits are used to bind and contain it, harnessing some fraction of its immense intellect for the creator’s use. The wisdom of doing so is questionable.

Other Entities

Hollow. A living body without a resonance echo. When a hollow is produced by the Lusk Reform Technique, they are called “the Reformed.” Usually dimwitted and easily controlled, though there are rumors of hollows that have been trained with skills beyond normal human limits.

Hull. A sparktech robot animated by a resonance echo. Note that most people don’t see resonance echoes as being intrinsic to their self-hood in the same way that a soul would be. The scientific consensus is that a resonance echo is just a psychic residue that accrues in the resonance field, and that its removal can calm violent or criminal impulses. Thus, most people view a hull as a utilitarian piece of hardware, no more alive or human than a toaster.

Possessed. A parasitic echo attachment. When resonance echoes manifest in the physical plane, they crave the resonance energy of the living, and must possess them to consume it. Multiple echoes may inhabit one body.

Vampire. An undying body animated and sustained by a resonance echo. When a parasitic echo bonds with its host body, via paranormal procedure, it becomes a vampire. The vampire’s own resonance echo diffuses fully into the physical body, giving it functional immortality while at the same time severing its link to the resonance field.

Vampires have been exceptionally rare for centuries, and are even more endangered due to the invention of the B.L.U.E. Array. The sunlight of bluetime burns and destroys them, so they must take great pains to avoid it.

A vampire sustains itself by feeding on resonance energy from a living being. In fables, vampires drank blood to accomplish this, but this is not typical. Each vampire feeds on energy in its own way—leaving a distinct telltale sign on its victim afterwards. A vampire can easily detect these signs in order to locate their victims again for feeding, or to take care to avoid feeding on the chosen prey of another vampire.

Magnitude

Paranormal entities and energies have a wide variety of effects and power levels. To help the GM judge these forces consistently, the following magnitude scale is provided. Magnitude measures the Quality level of an entity or anomaly: its area, scale, duration, range, and force. You can use the magnitude of an entity or power as a dice pool for a fortune roll to see how much effect it has, if it’s not obvious or certain.

Area / Scale

Force Examples

  • 0 A firm shove, breeze, tiny spark

Anomalies

An anomaly is a tear in the fabric of reality, a rift through which paranormal entities enter our dimension, and where the fundamental laws of reality no longer hold true. Anomalies can appear in a range of magnitudes.

Minor Anomaly: A thinning of reality in a particular location. The precursor to a proper tear. Often accompanied by uncanny sensations such as déjà vu, dizziness, or unusual temperatures. Paranormal activity is increased, and paranormal powers are heightened.

Standard Anomaly: A room-sized tear in reality, a rift that opens directly to the resonance field (or possibly a more distant dimension). Causes strong disturbances in its vicinity, up to a distance of a city block or more. Greatly increased paranormal activity, multiple resonance entities, etc.

Major Anomaly: A rift the size of a city block. Rare. The effects are felt districtwide, and are powerful in magnitude.

Mega Anomaly: A tear in reality the size of an entire district, or even multiple districts. Very rare. Quite likely to be a portal to distant dimensions, or other locations, historical eras, or timelines.

Some anomalies are off the scale, such as the cataclysm.

Anomaly Distortions

Anomalies can cause a wide range of localized reality distortions. These effects may disappear if the anomaly is sealed, but they may persist—sometimes permanently.

Anomalies can be stabilized and harnessed through paranormal experimentation, giving some measure of control over which distortions manifest through the anomaly, and how they manifest.

1 – 2

  • 1 Gravity: Super heavy gravity, less gravity, or no gravity at all.

  • 2 Time: Short loops, long loops, glimpses of past or future, fast aging, reverse aging.

  • 3 Magnetism: May cause metal objects to be crushed, fly together, etc.

  • 4 Dimensional portals: May be a large semi-stable portal to another place or time, or may be multiple small portals connected around the local area.

  • 5 Temperature: Sweltering heat, freezing cold, etc.

  • 6 Plant growth: Rapid, tangled spread of plant life.

3 – 4

  • 1 Tessellation: Upside-down staircases, recursive paths, impossible geometries, etc.

  • 2 Emotions: Induces an extreme emotional state in those nearby, such as rage, lust, euphoria, terror, etc.

  • 3 Light: Psychedelic colors, bright light, complete darkness, etc.

  • 4 Sound: Complete silence, or deafening loudness, or a random combination thereof.

  • 5 Spatial dimensions: Spaces that make no sense—rooms too large for their exterior, etc. People or objects may be scaled at random.

  • 6 Memory: Key memories may be altered or disappear entirely.

5 – 6

  • 1 Object permanence: Physical objects, or even body parts, may phase in and out of reality, disappearing and reappearing at random.

  • 2 Genetic coding: Rapid biological evolution. Physical mutations, whether subtle or radical.

  • 3 Weather: Localized meteorological phenomenon. Hurricanes, thick fog, thunderstorm, freezing blizzards, etc.

  • 4 Probability: Things that should not be possible become likely, or vice versa. Distortions of this kind have caused major problems in the casinos of Silkshore.

  • 5 Synaptic connection: Telepathic communication and the emergency of collective consciousness.

  • 6 Entropy: The degradation of matter and energy. Things rust, decay, and break down. Machines stop working.

Section 6 and the Death Radar

The Death Radar at Section 6 Central detects whenever someone dies in the city, and it sounds a klaxon. This is only heard by those near the site of death, and by the Reality Wardens , whose ceramic masks contain a special sparktech receiver attuned to the klaxon.

A team of mourners , hollows driving an unmarked car, use a resonance counter to find the body. They park outside, resonance counter crackling and clicking, and radio the precise location to Section 6 Central. A Reality Warden is dispatched to retrieve the body and bring it to the Bellweather Electroplasmic Fusion Station for processing.

After its resonance echo has been processed, the physical body is disposed of in a confidential but entirely respectful manner.

Reality Wardens are also responsible for securing and sealing anomalies. After locking down the surrounding area, they use large sparktech devices called Stability Anchors to dampen the anomaly’s effects and seal it entirely. They usually wipe the memory of any local witnesses after the anomaly has been secured.

Paranormal Playbooks

If your character dies, you may transfer to the Ghost/Echo playbook if you wish. As a PC who can resist any amount of harm, remember that your character’s death is unlikely to occur unless you want it to. If you want to try playing a resonance echo, you’ll have to help make it happen.

A Ghost/Echo may eventually transfer to a Hull or Vampire playbook, if they manage to complete the tasks necessary to become one of those entities. Building your own hull is a complex sparktech project. Preparing a hollow body for a vampire involves finding, learning, and performing an arcane paranormal experiment.

Sometimes you might introduce a Time Traveler , from either the past or the future. They come to ’68 for a specific purpose, and return to their own time after a short but eventful visit.

If you’d like to begin the game as a paranormal playbook, rather than first playing a standard playbook, talk to your game group about the idea and see what they think.

Ghost/Echo A RESONANCE ECHO ADRIFT WITHOUT A BODY

When you play a Ghost/Echo, you earn xp when you exact vengeance upon those whom you deem deserving, or when you express your outrage or anger. Get payback on those who wronged you, but watch out—Reality Wardens will hunt you down, and paranormalists may attempt to bind you.

Starting Actions: 1 Hunt, 1 Prowl, 1 Attune

Add all your current action ratings to the ratings on the Ghost/Echo playbook. You may not have an action rating greater than 4.

Remove any deadlocks, and consider changing your keys to reflect your new, vengeful disposition. Note down any iconic items you carried in life that still exist in your ghostly, glitched-out resonance form. Instead of contacts, note down only your enemies and rivals.

You keep any Echo special abilities from your human playbook; Echo Fighter , Echo Walker , etc. You lose all other special abilities. You start with the first trait on your playbook: Resonance Echo . You may also choose one more trait. (Ghost/Echoes may not choose Possess as a starting trait.)

Note: The traits on paranormal playbooks are not special abilities, and cannot be taken by living characters by using a veteran advance.

Drain

Instead of Stress, you now take Drain. You have 9 Drain boxes. Whenever you would take Stress, take Drain instead. When fully drained, strike out a key but choose an echo irregularity instead of a deadlock as normal. You cannot turn keys.

Echo Irregularities: Chaotic, Destructive, Furious, Obsessive, Territorial, Sadistic.

Need

You no longer have a vice. Instead you have an intense need : resonance energy. To satisfy this need, possess a living victim and consume their life essence (this may be a downtime action). When you do so, clear half your Drain (round down). You cannot possess victims at first—you have to advance to earn the ability.

Vampire

A RESONANCE ECHO ANIMATING AN UNDYING BODY

When you play a Vampire, you earn xp when you display your dominance or slay without mercy. You transcend the mortal world. Your concerns and needs eclipse all others.

Starting Actions: 1 Hunt, 1 Prowl, 1 Skirmish, 1 Attune, 1 Command, 1 Sway

If you transfer your character to the Vampire playbook, add all your current action ratings to the ratings on the Vampire playbook. You may not have an action rating greater than 4. You keep any Echo special abilities from your previous playbook. You lose all other special abilities. You start with the first trait on your playbook: Vampire . You may also choose one more Vampire trait.

Your deadlocks are maxed out. Choose four that reflect your vampiric nature. See the playbook sheet for more details.

Rebirth

Being reborn as a vampire is a radical transformation. While the occult ritual or paranormal experiment may be short, there will always be a period of weeks where the vampire acclimates to their new existence. You shun your former associates and live in the shadows, familiarizing yourself with your new form, while instinctively gathering dark servants and new material possessions. Consider a time skip of a few weeks, or play a temporary character in the meantime.

When the vampire returns, you have two Dark Servants and the Vampire items listed on the next page. Your former relationships, whether friends or rivals, mean little to you now.

Feeding

Your vice is resonance energy, consumed from a living human. Use 1 downtime activity to Hunt prey and clear Stress equal to the highest die. When you feed, mark four ticks on your healing clock (this is the only way you can heal). How do you feed? What telltale sign do you leave on your victims?

Strictures

When you gain a new Vampiric trait (except versatile), add a stricture. You start with the Dayfire stricture already.

  • Dayfire: When your skin is exposed to bluetime, take a new level 1 harm ( Burned ) every few minutes.

  • Slumber: In downtime, you must spend one activity resting in a dark, silent place (or else suffer 3 Stress).

  • Forbidden: You cannot enter a private residence without permission from the owner.

  • Repelled: Spiritbane charms can hold you at bay. (Take 2 Stress to resist the repulsion.)

  • Bestial: When you suffer physical harm or overindulge your vice, your body twists into a horrific bestial form until you next feed without overindulging.

  • Bound: Your spirit must remain in this body, or be destroyed.

Dark Servants

Start with two.

  • Blackwood , a chauffeur.

  • Sable , a fashion model.

  • Langdon , a biographer.

  • Carter , a bodyguard.

  • Green , a personal assistant.

Vampire Items

  • Fine clothes and accessories: An elegant outfit in a timeless style. Sure to intimidate. If you’re carrying this item as a second outfit to change into, it counts as 2 load. 0 load.

  • Fine personal weapon: Perhaps an ivory-handled silenced shooter, a gold stiletto, a cane-sword, a curvy antique dagger, a garrote hidden in a wristwatch, or an umbrella-sword with a groovy geometric pattern. 1 load.

  • Fine autopod: A luxury two-seat autopod, sleek and menacing. Imagine if an apex carnivore were a vehicle. 0 load.

  • Vial of a rare and exotic drug (x2): Very few have even heard of it. Where did you get it from? Choose an effect each time you produce a vial, or suggest something else: levitation, euphoria, shadow-sight, dream-walking, fake-death, bestial form, hypnosis. 0 load.

  • Demonbane charm: An antique arcane trinket that demons and other Class VI Entities prefer to avoid. 0 load.

  • Spiritbane charm: An antique arcane trinket that resonance echoes prefer to avoid. 0 load.

Time Traveler

A VOYAGER VISITING THIS ERA ON AN IMPORTANT MISSION

You are a visitor from the future, here on an important mission. Your mind starts muddled from the time travel process. Every time you fill your playbook xp, you gain clarity—choose a new Time Traveler trait , and a versatile ability from another playbook (reflecting your former life and experiences), and take another temporal glitch , and note down a new detail of your mission .

When you play a Time Traveler, you earn xp when you address a challenge with fearless conviction or lateral thinking. Time Travelers are powerful, but they’re here for a specific purpose, and once their mission is complete (or even before), they must return to their era and leave the crew behind. You burn brightly but briefly; make the most of your time here.

Unlike the other paranormal playbooks, an existing character does not typically transfer to this one. A player might wish to play a Time Traveler while their regular PC is in prison, or if they overindulge their vice and go on a bender, or if their character marks their last deadlock and has to retire. Alternatively, a player might want to mix things up and try the Time Traveler out, and their regular character simply sits on the bench while they do. There’s no right or wrong way to introduce the Time Traveler.

Starting Actions: Place nine dots (max three dots in any action), reflecting your personal history, experience, background, and heritage.

Why this crew? Why crime? All Time Travelers come to ’68 for an important mission, which might beg the question of why they choose to join a criminal crew and goof around on scores. You don’t have to answer this question right away, and should play to find out. But consider:

  • The crew matter more than they realize. The nature of campaign play, ticking timebombs, and the various playbooks means that the crew is almost always a pivotal piece in one or more world-changing events.

  • They might be related. Perhaps the Time Traveler is an ancestor or descendant of one or more PCs, or they were sent here by an ancestor or descendant.

  • It might be random chance. Perhaps the portal happens to open in the right place at the right time, and the Time Traveler has to make the best of the crew they’ve landed in.

Temporal Instability

Instead of Stress, you experience temporal instability. Restabilizing requires using a paranormal device powered by a canister of alluvium (downtime action). When you do this, clear 5 Instability (you cannot overindulge). Note that alluvium is rare and illegal. You arrive with four canisters of alluvium.

Temporal Glitches

When you mark your last point of Instability, strike out a key but choose a temporal glitch instead of a deadlock as normal. When you do, you recall a new detail of your mission (see the next section). Once you have four temporal glitches, you disappear from this time period entirely after the next score . Make it count.

Temporal Glitches: Body Phasing, Hallucinations, Lagging, Headaches, Flashbacks, Time Skips.

Time Traveler from the Future

Future Missions

Choose a mission—the reason you traveled back through time. Add a new detail when you gain a new temporal glitch.

  • Prevent a Terrible Future: You come from a future where things went bad; you’ve come back to stop that happening.

  • Chase Down Something Bad: Something or someone dangerous came here from your time, and you followed to stop them.

Future Items

  • Powerful mechanical limb: A sparktech arm or leg that improves your speed and strength. What’s it made of, and how did you end up with it? 0 load.

  • Force field device: Can be activated once per score to eliminate one instance of physical harm, completely resisting it. 1 load.

  • Fine future pistol: Subtle as a silenced shooter, but hits like a handcannon. Does it fire lasers, electromagnetically accelerated slugs, needles, rocketpropelled gyrojet rounds, cryo-capsules, soundwaves, or something else? 1 load.

  • Fine plasma blade: A melee weapon with a blade made of pure, humming energy. Can cut through steel like paper. 1 load.

  • Holo-projector: A handheld metal puck that can be activated once per score to project a three-dimensional image up to the size of an autopod. Very convincing at a distance, but doesn’t hold up to close inspection. 1 load.

  • Mysterious tape reel: You can’t remember what this is, yet—just that it’s critical to your mission. You can only define its purpose once, and when used, it’s gone forever. It comes from the future, so it could be anything; perhaps it hacks an electronic system, opens a sparktech door, or contains critical blueprints or vital footage. 0 load.

TIME TRAVELER FROM THE PAST

You are a visitor from the past, here on an important mission. The Time Traveler from the Past functions the same as the one from the Future (see previous page), experiencing the same temporal instability and glitches, and so on. They do have their own mission, and unique items, detailed here.

PAST MISSIONS

Choose a mission—the reason you traveled forward through time. Add a new detail when you gain a new temporal glitch.

  • Find a Solution and Take It Back: There’s a terrible problem in the past, and you’re here to find the fix and take it back.

  • Save the Present from a Past Mistake: Something terrible happened in your time that’s about to impact today. You’re here to stop it.

PAST ITEMS

  • ◆Fine shadow cloak: A hooded cloak made of rare Iruvian shadow-silk that blends into the darkness around it. This item improves your effect level when you sneak around. [1 load]

  • ◆Fine blade (Jira-forged): A blade made by the most famous weaponsmith of the last century. Perfectly balanced and deadly. Is it a deceptively simple fighting knife, or perhaps a broad Severosi dagger, a Daggerite stiletto, or an Iruvian saber? [1 load]

  • ◆Fine antiquated pistol: A breech-loading handgun. It may be antiquated, but the accuracy and stopping power are as impeccable as ever. Was it made by Kardera’s Daughters, Templeton & Slane, the Imperial Forge, or some other gunsmith? [1 load]

  • ◆Electroplasmic ammo: A case of electroplasmic ammo, especially potent against paranormal entities, but less effective against physical targets. The electrical charge is enough to stun a person, but does very little real harm. Several hits might incapacitate a human target. [1 load]

  • ◆Demonbane charm: An antique arcane trinket that demons and other Class VI Entities prefer to avoid. [0 load]

  • ◆Mysterious ghost key: You can’t remember what this is for, yet—just that it’s critical to your mission. You can only define its purpose once, and when used, it’s gone forever. Perhaps it opens a door, or a vault, or a device, or something more occult and metaphorical. [0 load]

340

Factions of Blades ’68

Faction Rolodex

Faction Rolodex

Each faction of Doskvol in 968AC is briefly described here, with detailed entries following for the criminal underworld, and the other most significant factions of the city.

Underworld

The Unseen (VI): A highly organized and powerful group with secret membership. Thought to pull the strings of most of Doskvol.

SCORPION Syndicate (IV): A massive and sophisticated Severosi drugsmuggling operation, mainly dealing in black lotus.

Strangford-Michter Financial (IV): A powerful investment firm with a habit of backing illegal ventures off the books. Has stakes in a large number of criminal groups and shady scientific projects.

Mirror House (III): Rogue scientists dedicated to the discovery and exploitation of metaphysical anomalies.

The Golden Hand (III): A high-tech criminal organization, known for using gold-plated hulls to do its dirty work.

The Tomorrow Program (III): A hedonistic association of notable scientists, writers, artists, performers, and dreamers. Lots of parties. Cover for extortion, graft, and murder.

The Gallo Family (II): A tight-knit criminal enterprise with deep roots in Silkshore. Gambling, loan sharking, and narcotics trafficking.

The Keel Twins (II): Identical twin sisters, ruthless gangsters, and minor nightlife celebrities. Many smaller gangs pay up to the Keels.

Lord Scurlock (II): An old recluse. Possibly a vampire. Obsessed with paranormal secrets.

Silk (II): A mysterious and legendary cat burglar, or possibly several of them. Every heist more ambitious than the last. Enjoys stealing the score from lesser thieves at the last moment.

The Blackjacks (II): The remnants of the former Rail Jacks union, who turned to crime when their services were replaced by robotic hulls. Paranormal specialists and underworld muscle for hire.

The Burn Corps (II): The radical militant arm of Burning Orange, the Severosi social activism collective. The Burn Corps is dedicated to armed self-defense, particularly against Bluecoat brutality.

The Limmerfield Gang (II): A tough gang with a reputation for brutality and strangeness.

The Lounge Rats (II): A seedy group of lounge lizards and nightclub owners. Extortion rackets, drug dealing, and illegal gambling.

Red Torque (II): A tight-knit gang of elite racing drivers who use their skills for criminal ends. Ambitious, angry, and fast.

D-13 (I): Ex-soldiers with no future, forced to pursue criminal opportunities.

The Dirt Barons (I): Freelance operators who dig up and trade dirt on other factions. Known for being honest brokers, they avoid crooked deals or double-crossing.

Leatherbacks (I): Rough and rowdy autocycle racers. A youth subculture as much as a gang. Named for their ubiquitous colorful leather jackets.

Paxton (I): A champion bare-knuckle boxer and a larger-than-life personality. Has spent more time inside Ironhook than out. Just got out on parole for the seventh time.

The Fighting Goats (I): A failed ragskulla team turned struggling criminal gang. Team uniforms, facepaint, ragskulla batons.

Fringe

New Dawn (IV): A fast-growing health and wellness cult, with various circles of membership. The commitments and rituals become more esoteric as you advance closer to the inner circle.

Carousel (III): A private research institute dedicated to finding the secret to eternal life.

The Kethrys Lodge (III): A society for those with a special interest in the paranormal. Conducts seances, experiments, and weird rituals.

The Silver Echoes (III): The most popular band in Doskvol. Detractors claim their horde of fans have been brainwashed by some combination of their electroplasmic instruments, the resonance field radios their music is broadcast over, and/or subliminal messages in their lyrics.

Old North Port Preservation Society (II): A sect of neo-traditionalists who have reclaimed Old North Port, beyond the safety of the Bubble. They believe that lost rituals and old runes keep them safe. Traditional robes, flower-gathering, effigy-burning.

Deathsteaders (I): Fugitives and eccentric free-thinkers that have left the safety of the Bubble to live in the deathlands, led by Daggerites accustomed to the eternal night.

Gondoliers (I): Most boats are now automated, but a handful of old gondoliers still work the canals. Said to know forgotten secrets of the dark.

Plasmovision Social Club (I):

Many homes now have plasmovision sets, beaming in the latest news and entertainment via the resonance field. Enthusiasts meet to discuss the latest shows and compare the mindwarping truths they see in the static between programs.

Institutions

Imperial Military (VI): Although the Age of Consensus is a “time of peace,” the Imperium is always at war. Garrisons are posted at Gaddoc Monorail Station, aboard the naval destroyer Nightcastle , and at the Premier’s stronghold (about 1,000 troops in total). The military also runs a training camp in Dunslough for fresh conscripts, along with a prison for draft-dodgers and deserters.

City Council (V): The elected rulers of the city government. Mostly aristocrats and nobles.

Section 6 (V): Responsible for the disposal of the dead, as well as enforcing Reality Constancy Law (investigating anomalies and unlicensed paranormal experimentation).

The Palace (V): The Imperial Intelligence Agency, or I.I.A. Spends as much time neutralizing internal threats to its own power as it does clashing with Blackthorn Park.

Ministry of Discovery (IV): Responsible for the research, acquisition, and application of new technologies in service to the Imperium.

Ministry of Stability (V): The governmental body of the Imperium that oversees border security, transportation between cities, and the disbursement of food and other vital resources.

The Strelai (IV): A secret sect of mystics with mysterious motives and affiliation.

The Church (IV): The Church of the Ecstasy of the Flesh. They honor the life of the body and deem the transcendent or otherworldly to be heretical. The Church promotes worship of the Immortal Crown (and the state).

Ironhook Prison (IV): A rusting relic of centuries past, where many scoundrels spend the bulk of their lives. In danger of being replaced by the Lusk Reform Technique.

Blackthorn Park (III): The foreign intelligence service of the United Skov Covenant. As Doskvol is the closest city to the border between Skovlan and Akoros, and contains a large population of Skovlanders, it’s a flash point in the covert war between Blackthorn Park and the Palace.

Bluecoats (III): The City Watch of Duskwall, tasked with upholding the law. Decades of PR have rebranded them as your friendly local constables, but they are still just as corrupt, violent, and cruel as ever.

Inspectors (III): The criminal investigators of the City Watch. They have a reputation for ethics and integrity (no one likes them). They present evidence for prosecutions to the city magistrates.

Lusk Reform Clinic (III): A small but well-funded facility near Ironhook Prison. Pioneers of the Lusk Reform Technique; they can turn the most violent criminal into one of the Reformed (vacant-eyed hollows) and also produce a brand-new hull to serve the city.

Anixis Field Unit (II): Iruvia has entered an isolationist period. Their intelligence agency (Anixis) maintains a small field unit in Doskvol to monitor the other spy agencies and advance Iruvian interests where possible.

Corporate & Community

The Foundation (IV): A powerful consortium of property developers, architects, and builders. Many of their enemies have disappeared behind the brick and mortar of the city.

Saltfords Banking Group (IV): After a wave of consolidations and acquisitions, Saltfords has survived as the largest and most powerful bank in Doskvol.

The Ankhayat Consortium (IV): A powerful association of businesses, all owned by an old-money family of exiled Iruvian nobility.

Burning Orange (III): A collective of indigenous activists from Severos, with a focus on social and environmental issues.

Shattered Isles Congress of Unions (SICU) (III): An international labor organization that fights for better working conditions, higher wages, social reforms, and the right to unionize.

Ink Lane Tabloids (II): The notorious tabloids of Doskvol: the Daily Scandal, the Evening Whisper, the Doskvol Lurker, News of the Void, and more. Buried amidst the weird and lurid trash are some surprisingly illuminating investigations.

Tottle Orlov Arran (II): Advertising agency. Won the contract to create all the posters, plasmovision adverts, and radio content for the official Doskvol Citizens Advice Bureau (a Ministry of Stability propaganda effort). Very well-connected.

The Youth Action Movement (II): A large countercultural protest group that started amongst the students of Charterhall University. The “Yammies” stage theatrical protests and sit-ins that involve performance art and spectacle, aimed at disrupting the Consensus in any way possible.

Cabbies (II): Autopod taxi drivers. An impressive gossip network.

Captain Laudius Blanchet (I): Former naval officer turned celebrity Void Sea explorer, filmmaker, and author. Runs the controversial (and dangerous) Void Sea Aquarium and Research Facility.

Xantara Pharm (IV): A profiteering pharmaceutical company that churns out pills of every kind and flavor—Dizzy Drops, Smiletime, Go-Go Pills, you name it.

Faction Details

Anixis Field Unit

Tier II (S)

Iruvia has entered an isolationist period. Their intelligence agency maintains a small field unit in Doskvol to monitor the other spy agencies and advance Iruvian interests.

Prestige Ability

Iruvian Precision: Add an Anixis wristwatch (0 load) to your playbook items; a state-of-the-art timepiece that includes a small laser cutter, grappling hook launcher, and remote detonator.

The Ankhayat Consortium

Tier IV (W)

A powerful association of businesses, all owned by an old-money family of exiled Iruvian nobility. 

Secure support for a coup,
from either Blackthorn
Park or the Palace

8

Provoke war between Iruvia and either the Imperium or 10 the USC

Turf The Iruvian Sands casino. Ankhayat Park. The Ankhayat Autodrome.

NPCs Eshala (first-born scion, ambitious , calculating , obsessed ). Harani (diplomat, charming , sharp , daring ). Rajan (casino manager, cold , enigmatic , conflicted ). Talya (racing driver, competitive , ruthless , loyal ).

Notable A deep treasury and many investments. The Iruvian Sands casino and Assets its income. Private security teams. Two large warships disguised as luxury superyachts. A luxury private airship with hidden weaponry.

Quirks The Imperium’s fleet lost all trace of the last leviathans many decades ago, but Iruvia’s charts and paranormal techniques proved effective for far longer. The Ankhayat Consortium has an unused ritual that can call the leviathans back, one last time. It is locked in a vault in case of emergency.

Allies Iruvian Consulate, Anixis Field Unit, The Gallo Family, Saltfords Banking Group. Enemies The Lounge Rats, The Unseen. Situation The Ankhayat family descend from the Iruvian noble house of the same name—their forebears once attempted to wrest control of that house and were exiled to Doskvol as punishment, generations ago. They have been plotting their revenge ever since. If they can gain the backing of one of the other great powers, they intend to push Iruvia into war via a false flag operation. They can then sweep in with the support of their allies and install themselves as rightful rulers. This plot is a tightly guarded secret, known only to the family.

Prestige Ability

The Spire of Khayat: A secret passage within the Iruvian Sands leads to a chamber directly beneath the towering obelisk of black glass that the casino is famous for. When you Indulge Vice here, you may choose to clear 5 Stress instead of rolling as normal. If you do, you receive a vision from Khayat.

The Blackjacks Tier II (W)

The remnants of the former Rail Jacks union, who turned to crime when their services were replaced by robotic hulls. Paranormal specialists and underworld muscle for hire.

Prestige Ability

Suit Up: Add an encounter suit with electro-field (2 load), lightning hook (1 load), spirit bottle (1 load), and breather mask (0 load) to your playbook items.

Blackthorn Park Tier III (S)

The foreign intelligence service of the United Skov Covenant. 

Rig Blueraker 5 to
explode

12 Complete City Council 10 infiltration

Turf Abandoned warehouse (HQ). Secret safe houses in every district. NPCs Ms. Asta (controller, brilliant , patient , cold ). Agent 2 (deceased field agent turned resonance echo, meticulous , paranoid , tired ). Agent 36 (field agent Rahana Hakar, daring , ambitious , loyal) . Notable Cutting-edge equipment. Weapon caches in hidden safe houses. Assets A large spy ring of informers planted at all levels of Imperial institutions. Quirks Blackthorn Park (named for their headquarters in Lockport) reports directly to the High Coven of the USC, and all agents are trained in parapsychology and psychic combat techniques. Allies Iruvian Consulate, Skov Citizens, City Council, Anixis Field Unit, The Burn Corps, Burning Orange. Enemies The Palace, Imperial Military, Ministry of Stability, Section 6, Ministry of Discovery. Situation Blackthorn Park has spent years planting agents throughout the city who siphon government secrets back to Skovlan. Having recently learned of the top secret rocket ship Blueraker 5, they’re now under urgent orders to find it and rig it to explode on its maiden voyage.

Prestige Ability

Covenant Training: If your character slips away to Skovlan for a few weeks to attend a training camp, they come back with the Echo Fighter special ability (Veteran playbook; if you already have it, pick something else appropriate).

Bluecoats Tier III (S)

The City Watch of Duskwall. Rebranded as friendly local constables, but they are still just as corrupt, violent, and cruel as ever. 

Patrol hulls replace
riotcoats

Riotcoats discredit 6 8 the patrol hulls

Turf The Bluecoats claim the whole city as their turf. Riotcoats dominate the poorer areas, and the wealthy areas are increasingly the domain of patrol hulls.

NPCs Commander Kinclaith (chief commissioner of the City Watch, vain , corrupt , cowardly ). Captain Gant (Public Order Unit operations commander, insecure , swaggering , bullying ). Superintendent Clermont (civilian head of the patrol hull division, reserved , calculating , cold ).

Notable Smiling thugs in uniform. High-speed instigator autopods. Canal Assets patrol boats. Riotcoats with heavy armor and weaponry. Patrol hulls (law enforcement automatons with built-in crowd control weaponry). Quirks The various espionage organizations all exploit the Bluecoats in their little games, playing them like pawns. Informants are rife within the ranks, and the place leaks information like a sieve. Allies City Council, Ironhook Prison, Lord Scurlock, The Unseen, The Limmerfield Gang, Imperial Military, The Church. Enemies Ministry of Stability, SICU, many criminal organizations. Situation The Ministry of Stability is rolling out patrol hulls outfitted for law enforcement. The riotcoats from the Public Order Unit are sensitive about their jobs being replaced by automatons, and they’ve started a secret campaign of sabotage in retaliation.

Prestige Ability

Snitch: Your crew takes -2 Heat every downtime. Deadlock a key when you unlock this ability.

The Burn Corps Tier II (W)

The radical militant arm of Burning Orange, a Severosi collective. The Burn Corps is dedicated to armed self-defense, particularly against Bluecoat brutality. 

Kick the Bluecoats out of
Charhollow

8 Radicalize Burning Orange

10

Turf Several city blocks and apartment buildings in Charhollow. NPCs Skaykay (ex-army general, leader of the Burn Corps, resourceful , keen , charismatic ). Professor Kole (spokesperson, passionate , principled , righteous ). Kaya (leviathan song, cryptic , all-knowing , calculating , maternal ).

Notable A large number of dedicated members, every one of them well-armed, Assets well-read, and disciplined. A cache of ex-military weaponry. Quirks The Burn Corps holds to certain aspects of Severosi mysticism. They practice the traditional martial arts of that region, and their leadership, while hard-headed and practical, receives guidance from a spiritual source (the song of a leviathan; heard, but never seen). Allies Burning Orange, Severosi Consulate, The Blackjacks, D-13, Blackthorn Park, SICU, The Dirt Barons.

Enemies City Council, Strangford-Michter Financial, Bluecoats.

Situation The Burn Corps’ armed patrols are designed to keep ordinary Severosi citizens safe, and push the corrupt Bluecoats out. They’ve already secured parts of Charhollow, and they want the whole district. The Burn Corps receives vital funding from Burning Orange, but there’s a deep tension over their methods. Does the Burn Corps go too far, or does Burning Orange not go far enough?

Prestige Ability

Deadly Weapon: Add fine martial art technique (0 load) to your playbook items. What Severosi fighting style do you master?

Burning Orange Tier III (W)

A collective of indigenous activists from Severos, with a focus on social and environmental issues.

Reclaim spiritual lands 8 Advance civil rights legislation 8 (repeating) (repeating)

Turf Deep roots in Charhollow, with a strong presence in Charterhall. The Severosi consulate building (HQ).

NPCs Kord (lawyer, walks with a cane, perceptive , knowledgeable , cutting ). Boudorna (nurse who heads up public education and women’s health workshops, understanding , quiet , loyal ). Felingo (deathlands researcher, clumsy , ambitious , romantic ). Notable Well-connected sympathizers and a few wealthy benefactors. Legislative Assets expertise, and a finger on the pulse of public opinion. A recent win granted official government recognition to two Severosi tribes (granting them aid and special tax status). Quirks Strangford-Michter Financial have made several attempts to infiltrate the faction in order to undermine it, only to be discovered and burned orange, discarded. Allies The Burn Corps, Severosi Consulate, The Tomorrow Program, Blackthorn Park, Inspectors. Enemies City Council, The Church, Strangford-Michter Financial, The Palace. Situation Burning Orange has been purchasing prime real estate and turning it into public project spaces such as hostels, accessibility workshops, and communal gardens, drawing the ire of several corporations. Their victories in the courts have also garnered the attention of the Imperial government. The crown hesitates to act directly against the organization, fearing public outcry, but it’s only a matter of time.

Prestige Ability

Dedicated: As an insider, you can donate 1 stack to Burning Orange to earn 1 Rep for your crew and put one tick on one of Burning Orange’s faction clocks. You can also work on Burning Orange’s faction clocks with your own Long-Term Project downtime activity.

Carousel Tier III (W)

A private research institute dedicated to finding the secret to eternal life. 

Recapture Test Subject Nimbus

8

Turf Carousel Labs, a large and secure complex in Coalridge (HQ). Array D, a facility outside the Bubble where Carousel’s most secret experiments take place. NPCs Perriston (research director, unorthodox , obsessive, sycophantic ). Seresh (research diagnostician, curious , absent-minded , brilliant ). Krumb (lab tech, quiet , principled , observant ). Notable Plenty of funding and high-tech equipment. A handful of test Assets subjects with powerful paranormal abilities. A squad of Chasers in powered armor, tasked with tracking down Runners (escaped test subjects). Allies Strangford-Michter Financial, City Council, Mirror House, Whitecrown Citizens, The Lounge Rats, Ministry of Discovery. Enemies New Dawn, Section 6, Xantara Pharm. Situation Most of Carousel’s test subjects die during experimentation. Some that survived have developed paranormal abilities that Carousel exploits, but they have not achieved anything close to immortality. That is, until now—Test Subject Nimbus is believed to exhibit true immortality. But they’ve become a Runner, and so far Carousel hasn’t been able to catch them.

Prestige Ability (choose one)

  • Chaser Training: Take +1 to Prowl , and armor is 2 load for you instead of 3.

  • Volunteer Test Subject: You return from Array D with either telekinesis, telepathy, pyrokinesis, or something other paranormal power. When you push yourself , you can invoke this power; the larger the magnitude you want to have, the more desperate the associated action will be. Deadlock one key immediately, as a side effect of the experiments. When you have deadlocked four keys, your character dies (rather than retiring).

The Church Tier IV (W)

The Church of the Ecstasy of the Flesh. They honor the life of the body and deem the transcendent or otherworldly to be heretical. The Church promotes worship of the Immortal Crown (and the state). 

Plant a rowan sapling inside the Bubble generator

10

Turf The Sanctorium grand cathedral in Brightstone. Many other temples. NPCs The Rowan Tree (Class VI Entity, enigmatic , petty, cruel ). Apex Oric (second, ambitious , devoted , hypocritical ).

Notable A large treasury of tithes from citizens. Many cohorts of acolytes who Assets enforce the will of the Church’s leadership. The Rowan Tree, huge and miraculous with vibrant green leaves, growing at the heart of the Sanctorium.

  • Quirks For the masses, church dogma is that your only eternal legacy will be your life’s work, and so you must work hard. Human achievement is glorified, and the Church especially venerates the Immortal Emperor (along with a slate of luminaries from business and politics). Assemblies involve a combination of lectures, admonishments, sacramental narcotics, and ecstatic trance. Sometimes ice cream. For the elites, the Church offers total hedonism, exploring the very limits of pleasure and pain.

Allies City Council, The Palace, Tottle Orlov Arran, Bluecoats.

  • Enemies The Kethrys Lodge, Leatherbacks, Ministry of Discovery, The Lounge Rats, Ink Lane Tabloids, The Silver Echoes, The Strelai, New Dawn, Burning Orange.

  • Situation The Church is secretly controlled by a Class VI Metadimensional Entity—the Rowan Tree. It wishes to plant a “sapling” of itself inside the Bellweather Electroplasmic Fusion Station, massively extending its influence across time and space. Should it achieve this, it will manipulate history. Create a new Tier 5 faction, Rowan House , that has been retroactively weaving a web of worldwide control for centuries. They have loyal undercover agents placed everywhere. One PC without an existing backing faction gains a new one immediately—Rowan House. They find a signet ring with the emblem of a rowan tree on their finger.

Prestige Ability

Mark of Authority: The back of your hand is branded with the symbol of the Church. Gain potency when you Sway or Consort with members of the faithful. When you deal with a group or faction, the GM will tell you who among them are church members (one, a few, many, or all).

City Council Tier V (S)

The elected rulers of the city government. Mostly aristocrats and nobles. 

Convert another council
member (repeating)

4 Expose the Charterhall Two

Turf The City Council chambers are in Charterhall, along with the attendant government offices and impregnable city treasury vaults. NPCs The Charterhall Two ( Clelland and Tyrconnell ). Also Strangford (wealthy scion, charming , arrogant , relaxed ), Penderyn (media mogul, ambitious , flamboyant , calculating ), Hellyers (weapon manufacturing magnate, amoral , avaricious , perceptive ), and Bowmore . Notable A massive treasury in gold, cash, and investments. Many officials, Assets barristers, clerks, and functionaries. The infrastructure of the city. Many units of city sanitation and maintenance hulls. Bodyguard hulls assigned to each councilor. Quirks Two members of the Council are also members of the Unseen, separate from any ties to Blackthorn Park. Allies Bluecoats, Blackthorn Park, Carousel, The Church, Lord Scurlock, The Foundation, The Unseen, Ministry of Discovery, Ministry of Stability. Enemies The Palace, Inspectors, Imperial Military, The Keel Twins, Anixis Field Unit, The Burn Corps, Burning Orange, SICU, The Youth Action Movement. Situation The City Council has been infiltrated by Blackthorn Park. The Charterhall Two work for the Skov spy agency, and they want unanimous control. To that end, they’re working on the holdouts—to either convert them to their cause through persuasion or blackmail, or have them taken out and replaced by more sympathetic councilors.

Prestige Ability

Traffic Routing: Once per score, you can request help (via phone, radio, or flashback). The City Council will use their powers to slow or stop a target in the streets (through roadworks, traffic jams, closed canals, public parades, etc.), and/or open a route to you.

D-13 Tier I (S)

Ex-soldiers with no future, forced to pursue criminal opportunities.

Establish a fortified sanctuary

8

Prestige Ability

Comrades: Call D-13 for help (by radio or flashback) and either Kobb will help out with sniper support, or the rest of the gang can deliver a truckload of high-powered explosives. The next time you make a Return the Favor roll for this backing faction, add +1d.

Deathsteaders

Tier I (S)

Fugitives and eccentric free-thinkers that have left the safety of the Bubble to live in the deathlands, led by Daggerites accustomed to the eternal night. 

Obtain strange artifacts
(repeating)

8

Connect to the 12 Serpentine Paths

Prestige Ability

Moonlit Path: Add lunar chalk to your playbook items (0 load). You can bridge locations together through the ghost field. Take 2 Stress to momentarily open a portal between locations marked with your lunar chalk. You can freely travel or see through the portal. Take 1 Stress for each additional feature: bring another person with you—send an object through on its own—leave the portal open for several minutes rather than moments .

The Dirt Barons

Tier I (S)

Freelance operators who acquire and trade dirt on other factions. With a reputation for being honest brokers, they try to avoid crooked deals or double-crossing.

Prestige Ability

Pay Dirt: When you use “share relevant intel with you” faction help, the Dirt Barons add +2 to their Tier for the gather information roll. Additionally, every score has an additional Called Shot just for your PC—acquire dirt. The GM will outline what the target intel is for a given score. If you get it, pocket +2 stacks as your cut of the Dirt Barons’ deal. This payout is for your PC, not the whole crew.

The Fighting Goats Tier I (W)

A failed ragskulla team turned struggling criminal gang.

Prestige Ability

Pinch Hitters: Put in a call and the Fighting Goats will show up to help; five keen scoundrels ready to go to bat. The next time you make a Return the Favor roll for this backing faction, add +1d.

The Gallo Family Tier II (S)

A tight-knit criminal enterprise with deep roots in Silkshore. Gambling, loan sharking, and narcotics trafficking.

Free Little Gallo 6 Get payback

8

Prestige Ability

High Roller: If you Indulge Vice in one of the Gallo Family’s casinos, you may adjust the dice outcome by 1 or 2 (up or down). An ally who joins in your vice may do the same.

The Golden Hand Tier III (S)

A Daggerite syndicate with an army of gold-plated hulls, operated behind a funeral home front. They serve the dead before the living, following the will of vengeful, revenant echoes.

Prestige Ability

Golden Ticket: When you die, if your body is brought to the Golden Hand, they’ll transfer your resonance echo to a hull immediately. Switch to the Hull playbook, keeping your previous action ratings. Immediately take one Hull playbook ability, and fill two memory clocks (choose the associated special abilities from your previous character’s abilities instead of rolling randomly). To remember more will require filling more memory clocks, as usual. Your owning faction is the Golden Hand, but they never mark suspicion; doing work for them is voluntary.

The Keel Twins Tier II (S)

Identical twin sisters, ruthless gangsters, and nightlife celebrities. Many smaller gangs pay up to the Keels.

Prestige Ability

A-List: When you make a lifestyle quality fortune roll, add this faction’s Tier in addition to each full row of bank (10 stacks) you have.

The Kethrys Lodge Tier III (W)

A society for those with a special interest in the paranormal. Conducts seances, experiments, and weird rituals. 

Acquire the Heart of Kotar

Situation The occult secrets of the past have been well-buried by the state and by the Consensus, and the Kethrys Lodge knows only a little about the mysteries and dangers of the paranormal powers they meddle with. One of the resonance entities they have contact with, a Class VI Metadimensional Entity known as Azarax, has directed them to find something called the Heart of Kotar. Although they know nothing about who or what Kotar is, or the potential dangers involved, they are determined to complete the task.

Prestige Ability

Bound to the Demon: Having bound yourself by arcane ritual to Azarax, when you indulge its dark desire, you receive permission to command it and it may not refuse you. When the demon’s desire is not well-satisfied, it receives permission to work in secret toward your destruction to be free of its binding.

Leatherbacks Tier I (W)

Rough and rowdy autocycle racers. A youth subculture as much as a gang. Named for their ubiquitous colorful leather jackets.

Centralize power under Brace 6 Kill Brace, and freedom reigns 8

Prestige Ability

Killer Ride: Add fine autocycle (0 load) to your playbook items; one big plasmic turbine engine on two wheels. Incredibly fast, incredibly dangerous.

The Limmerfield Gang Tier II (W)

A tough gang with a reputation for brutality and strangeness. 

Find Mother

6 Release Mother

12

Prestige Ability

Terrifying Reputation: When you Command a frightened target, take +1d . When you deal with a group or faction, the GM will tell you who among them are afraid of the Limmerfield Gang (one, a few, many, or all).

Lord Scurlock Tier II (S)

An old recluse. Possibly a vampire. Obsessed with paranormal secrets. 

Rescue Setarra

12

Prestige Ability

Extensive Library: When you gather information on the arcane or paranormal, or perform a paranormal experiment, take bonus dice equal to Lord Scurlock’s Tier.

The Lounge Rats Tier II (W)

A seedy group of lounge lizards and nightclub owners. Extortion rackets, drug dealing, and illegal gambling.

Sabotage the 8 Live forever, baby B.L.U.E. Array

12

Prestige Ability

Encore: If you die and your body is taken to the Lounge Rats, they will bring you back to life. This will take some time, represented by a clock. Every personal business phase, make a fortune roll on behalf of the Lounge Rats as per a LongTerm Project downtime activity (other characters can help). The GM decides the size of this clock based on the pacing of your campaign, anywhere from a single personal business phase with no roll needed, up to a 10-segment clock. When this is complete, your character comes back—switch to the Vampire playbook, keeping your original action ratings and special abilities.

Ministry of Discovery Tier IV (S)

The governmental body responsible for the research and acquisition of new technologies in service to the Imperium. 

Launch Blueraker 5

10

Prestige Ability

Rocket Pack: Add a rocket pack (2 load) to your playbook items; an experimental device that can be hidden in a briefcase. Strap it on and you can fly, at least until the fuel runs out.

Ministry of Stability Tier IV (S)

The governmental body of the Imperium that oversees border security, transportation between cities, and the disbursement of food and other vital resources. 

Expand nutriwafer
Distribution

8 Roll out the Model Worker Initiative 10 to a new district (repeating)

Prestige Ability

High Security Clearance: You have an open license to leave the Bubble, and can take free first-class passage (with guests) by rail to anywhere else in the Imperium.

Mirror House

Tier III (S)

Rogue scientists dedicated to the discovery and exploitation of metaphysical anomalies.

Dominate the
alluvium trade
6
Extract secrets at the
Blue Baths (repeating)
4
Turf
Fine old manor house, revealing a mind-bending complex of
illusionary perspectives, orthogonal gravity, and tessellating
staircases on the inside (HQ). Scientists and paranormalists in their
service.
NPCs
Karstas(lead experimentalist,brilliant,possessed,enigmatic).
Ixis-9(sparkmind construct,alien,obsessive,scheming).
Assets
A private electroplasmic fusion device and resonance entity
containment vault. Many resonance entities bound to service.
Experimental reality-bending gadgets and weapons.
Quirks
All members of Mirror House dress in metallic catsuits and reflective
masks to protect their identities. Are they possibly related to the Dimmer
Sisters, the Reconciled, the Path of Echoes, or some other ancient faction?
Allies
Carousel, The Kethrys Lodge, Strangford-Michter Financial,
The Tomorrow Program, New Dawn.
Enemies
Section 6, Gondoliers.
Situation
Mirror House has been slowly and methodically consolidating the
trade of alluvium (the concentrated matter of the resonance field,
quantum instability in physical form). Why they need so much
alluvium is unknown.
Their other scheme involves the Blue Baths, a bathing and leisure
complex in New Horizon. The complex has a mirrored structure below
ground that uses experimental technology and a harnessed anomaly
to monitor the resonance echoes of those in the baths above, and
extracts their secrets in a dreamlike state. The victims are usually
none the wiser. Mirror House sells these secrets to the highest bidder.

Prestige Ability

Perfect Reflection: The death or possession of your physical body instantly frees your resonance echo. Your spirit steps out of one of the mirrors in Mirror House. Switch to the Ghost/Echo playbook and immediately take an additional playbook advancement.

New Dawn Tier IV (W)

A fast-growing health and wellness cult, with various circles of membership. The commitments and rituals become more esoteric as you advance closer to the innermost circle.

Acquire void key (repeating) 6 Free the Red Queen

12

Prestige Ability

The Closing of the Eye: You no longer suffer terror from the paranormal, and can choose to take only 1 Stress when you resist mental, electroplasmic, or spiritual harm, regardless of the result of your roll.

Old North Port Preservation Society Tier II (W)

A sect of neo-traditionalists who have reclaimed Old North Port, beyond the safety of the Bubble. They believe that lost rituals and old runes keep them safe. Traditional robes, flower-gathering, effigy-burning.

Prestige Ability

Glory to the Harvest: When you make a human sacrifice as part of a paranormal experiment, its magnitude is boosted by one level, and it costs 3 less Stress.

The Palace

Tier V (S)

The Imperial Intelligence Agency. Spends as much time neutralizing internal threats to its own power as it does clashing with Blackthorn Park. 

Put asset in charge of
Section 6

12 Expose Blackthorn Park agents 8 (repeating)

Turf Cairn House (Doskvol I.I.A. headquarters). Various temporary, clandestine field offices and interrogation rooms. A black site outside the Bubble. NPCs Prichard (handler, disheveled , perceptive , cynical ). Baine (spy, charming , candid , manipulative ). Thistle (assassin, cold , efficient , dedicated ). Assets Advanced sparktech espionage equipment. Many disposable assets and moles, scattered throughout the city. Quirks Powerful and ruthless, the Palace operates with a generous budget and almost no oversight or accountability for its actions. Allies Imperial Military, The Church, The Unseen, Red Torque, Anixis Field Unit. Enemies Blackthorn Park, City Council, Section 6, Iruvian Consulate, Dagger Isles Consulate, Inspectors, Burning Orange, SICU. Situation The Palace is always busy with the usual game of cat-and-mouse with Blackthorn Park. Beyond that, current orders from the top are to eliminate the Master Warden of Section 6, and further ensure that they are replaced with a loyal Palace asset.

Prestige Ability

Advanced Chronometer: Add a Palace wristwatch (0 load) to your playbook items. The bezel spins, acting like a small circular saw. It also contains a powerful electroplasmic field generator that can deflect bullets; once per score, you can use the field to reduce or avoid physical harm, instead of rolling to resist.

Paxton Tier I (S)

A champion bare-knuckle boxer and a larger-than-life personality. Has spent more time inside Ironhook than out. Just got out on parole for the seventh time.

Prestige Ability

Big Night Out: If you Indulge Vice drinking with Paxton, add 4 to the dice outcome to determine how much Stress you clear. An ally who joins in your vice may do the same. If you overindulge, pick two consequences instead of one, and the crew marks 2 Rep.

Red Torque Tier II (W)

A tight-knit gang of elite racing drivers who use their skills for criminal ends. Ambitious, angry, and fast.

Prestige Ability

Afterburner: Bring a vehicle to their secret chopshop (ahead of time or in a flashback) and they’ll outfit it with one of their custom Red Torque afterburners. Activate it once per score for a temporary burst of explosive acceleration.

SCORPION Syndicate Tier IV (W)

A massive and sophisticated Severosi drug-smuggling operation, mainly dealing in black lotus. 

Subordinate rival
smugglers

8 Establish processing facility

12

Prestige Ability

Paint the House: If requested, SCORPION will send a couple of autopods full of armed goons (about 12 total, a medium gang) to help you out during a score. The next time you make a Return the Favor roll for this backing faction, add +1d.

Section 6

Tier V (S)

Responsible for the disposal of the dead, as well as enforcing Reality Constancy Law (investigating anomalies and unlicensed paranormal experimentation).

Prestige Ability

Warden Training: Gain the Medium ability (Paranormalist playbook; if you already have it, pick something else appropriate), and add fine Warden mask (0 load) to your playbook items.

Shattered Isles Congress of Unions (SICU) Tier III (W)

An international labor organization that fights for better working conditions, higher wages, social reforms, and the right to unionize.

Sniff out the spies 6 Trigger the general strike and agitators (repeating)

10

Turf Local headquarters in Barrowcleft. Workers’ halls across the city. NPCs Becker (Doskvol chapter president, bold , passionate , decisive ). Holt (Doskvol chapter treasurer, diligent , dedicated , shy ). Assets Many proud members across several industries—factory workers, service workers, cabbies, dockers, and more. Quirks SICU is vigorously opposed to hulls and the replacement of human labor with that of machines. Nevertheless, the Doskvol chapter’s treasurer, Holt, is secretly a hull (with a very lifelike appearance). Only Becker, the president, knows. Allies The Blackjacks, The Burn Corps, D-13, The Youth Action Movement, Charhollow Citizens, Coalridge Citizens, Cabbies, Dockers, Laborers, Service Workers. Enemies Strangford-Michter Financial, Xantara Pharm, Ministry of Stability, Bluecoats, The Palace, City Council.

Situation SICU has always suffered from heavy interference by multiple spy agencies, whether trying to mobilize the faction for their own ends, or to hinder the faction’s efforts on behalf of big business and the state. SICU has become very good at sniffing out spies. Beyond this, they’re working toward the largest general strike in Imperial history. Their demands would revolutionize working conditions and social equality across society.

Prestige Ability

Union Strong: Gain +1d and potency when you Consort with fellow union members. In any scene, the GM will tell you who among any workers present are union members (one, a few, many, or all).

Silk

Tier II (S)

A mysterious and legendary cat burglar, or possibly many of them. Every heist more ambitious than the last. Enjoys stealing the score from lesser thieves at the last moment. 

Recruit and train a new
Silk

8 Rob the city treasury vaults

12

Prestige Ability

Silk III: Bell can help out during heists; take the Dead Drop ability (Operative playbook; if you already have it, pick something else appropriate). If you haven’t made your entrance to a score yet, you can nominate any minor NPC and reveal that they’re actually you, in disguise.

Strangford-Michter Financial Tier IV (S)

A powerful investment firm with a habit of backing illegal ventures off the books. Has stakes in a large number of criminal groups and shady scientific projects.

Fabricate incriminating info to discredit the unions 8 Establish new pharmaceutical brand 10

Prestige Ability

Seed Funding: At the end of each personal business phase, you earn +3 bank . If you ever fall out with Strangford-Michter, they will want to recoup their investment…

The Strelai Tier IV (S)

A secret sect of mystics with mysterious motives and affiliation.

Prestige Ability

Secrets of the Balance: You know secret rituals by which you may attempt to summon, command, or bind Class VI Metadimensional Entities. In your hands, meteoric metal blazes with paranormal energy and can cut through anything.

The Tomorrow Program

Districts of Doskvol

Barrowcleft

After the Bubble was created and Bowmore Fields established, Barrowcleft converted from agricultural production to an industrial hub. The radiant energy farms were sold to property developers and became the new housing subdivision of Radiance. What remains of Barrowcleft is now dominated by canneries, bottling plants, and processed food factories. Raw produce comes from Bowmore Fields to be turned into branded consumer goods. A burgeoning middle class collects a steady paycheck from these factories, and the old wide roads of Barrowcleft now bustle with a steady stream of autopod traffic.

Landmarks

Goffin’s Fantastic Fireworks.

Doskvol’s premiere fireworks factory. Goffin’s pyrotechnic entertainments are enjoyed by rich and poor alike, and the mascot (a jolly skeleton child) is seen on billboards across the city. This factory has a terrible health and safety record, but hasn’t burned down for more than 11 months now.

Derelict Lightning Tower.

The Bubble made the lightning towers obsolete decades ago. Most of the decommissioned towers have been taken down, but a few remain. Barrowcleft’s tower is a popular meeting spot for local Leatherback racers and teenage hoodlums. They’ve hacked the old generator, and at night they play deadly games of chicken under crackling 388 arcs of electroplasmic lightning.

3 Vale Farm. Whether eagerly or with reluctance, the proud old families of Barrowcleft have sold their land. Only one now remains—the Vale farm. This archaic radiant energy farm, hemmed in by apartment blocks and factories, struggles to compete with the new farms of Bowmore Fields. The Vales have been around a long time, though, and they hold a lot of sway in Barrowcleft.

4 Barrowcleft Drag. The primary commercial strip in Barrowcleft. The old open-air markets have been replaced by an assortment of foodmarts, milkbars, autopod garages, diners, department stores, and gamblingmachine halls. Commerce is booming, and this wealth has yet to be fully exploited by criminal elements.

Details

Traits

Scene: Roads busy with hundreds of autopods, workers commuting to their factory jobs. A door-to-door salesperson carrying a briefcase full of spirit essence. A carefully stacked pyramid of canned goods outside a foodmart. A gang of Leatherbacks roaring past on their autocycles.

Streets: Wide paved roads. Main Road , Sweetmilk Lane , Cannery Drive.

Buildings: Low, wide buildings. Sprawling factories. Stores and diners with modern, sweeping architecture made of glass and steel. Vending machines and automats.

Notables

Wealth ●●● ● Security & Safety ●● ●● Criminal Influence ● ●●● Paranormal Activity ●●●●

The junkyards and chop shops in Barrowcleft are the best place in the city for parts and modifications. Take +1d to acquire an asset or work on a long-term project related to vehicles, but take +1 Heat (the Bluecoats keep an eye on these operations).

Peggy Vale. Matriarch of the Vale clan. The Vales have started smuggling and running protection rackets to stay afloat. ( Proud, Fierce, Stubborn )

Dobb Barstow. Crooked entrepreneur whose latest scam is a popular pyramid scheme selling a wellness product they call “spirit essence.” ( Confident, Delighted, Cold-Blooded )

Frank Mayberry. The tough old customer who runs the biggest junkyard and garage in Barrowcleft. ( Grumpy, Knowledgeable, KindHearted )

Shady Factions & Ward Bosses

Strangford-Michter Financial (p. 380), Red Torque (p. 375), Leatherbacks (p. 364)

Bowmore Fields

The creation of the Bubble greatly expanded the Doskvol city limits, opening up new tracts of land for the first time in centuries. Most of this land, dubbed Bowmore Fields, was given to the Ministry of Stability for the purpose of intensive high-production agriculture. Radiant farming is a thing of the past; experimental new plants are grown here that thrive on just their daily allotment of bluetime.
