The farms of Bowmore Fields bring in a lot of money, and boast state-of-the-art equipment and high-tech security systems. Contract workers live in eerie little towns that are isolated from the bustle of the city proper.

Landmarks

Nutriwafer Processing Facility.

The farms of Bowmore Fields cannot feed the entire booming population of Doskvol. To avoid a famine, the Ministry manufactures and distributes nutriwafer. It’s a complete nutritional meal in one dry mouthful, freely available to the needy. The processing facility is one of the most heavily guarded buildings in Doskvol; tall iron walls bristle with sensors and automated security. Section 6 vans roll in, distribution trucks roll out.

Dawncrest Heavy Equipment

Control Center. Several squads

of laborer hulls, a product of the Lusk Reform Technique, work across the district. Their grimy, battered frames are stored in this large industrial lot. Here they are repaired, assigned to 390 jobs, and eventually decommissioned when they wear out.

Renascent Forest. This district

was part of the deathlands until recently, a wild and haunted landscape. Most of that wilderness was cleared for farmland when the Bubble was created, but some of the old forests remain. Nobody is sure why—was the reason well-meant nature conservation, or something more sinister? Whatever the case, the depths of these woods are dark, foreboding, and best avoided.

Monitory Peak Coronagraph. A

private research facility at the top of the highest peak of Doskvol. The coronagraph probes the sky to learn the secrets of the wild blue yonder. Is Tycheros out there? What is it, and where is it? When is it? What else might be in the Blue? Who funds this facility, and for what purpose? The place raises more questions than it answers.

Details

Traits

Scene: An armored Section 6 van rumbling past on its way to the Nutriwafer Processing Facility. Laborer hulls marching down a dusty road to their next assignment. Locals in a diner giving some outsiders a hard stare. Rough sleepers camped in a ditch, burning fence posts for warmth.

Streets: Dusty, wide, and quiet. Assurance Drive , Dayspring Road , Mount Monitory Tunnel , the A2.

Buildings: Towering agricultural structures; grain elevators, processing facilities, and spraying rigs. Modular chrome outbuildings. Cheap prefabricated houses for contract workers (cold and damp).

Notables

Wealth ●● ●● Security & Safety ●● ●● Criminal Influence ●●●● Paranormal Activity ●● ●●

This is a big, sparsely populated, isolated district—only take +1 Heat if killing was involved in a score here (rather than +2 as usual). Operations against the Ministry of Stability in Bowmore Fields are considered on “hostile turf” for the purpose of generating Heat.

Lynthia Axton. Bowmore Fields Head of Oversight, appointed by the Ministry of Stability. Responsible for assigning contracts and auditing farms. ( Meticulous, Patient, Hard )

Doctor Hungate. Chief Scientist at a top secret Ministry farm where experimental crops and livestock are tested. ( Brilliant, Uncompromising, Cryptic )

The Scarecrow. A laborer hull who broke their programming and escaped. Blamed for various acts of sabotage across Bowmore Fields. ( Vengeful, Scared, Alone )

Shady Factions & Ward Bosses

SCORPION Syndicate (p. 376), Red Torque (p. 375)

Brightstone

Doskvol’s epicenter of wealth and power. Brightstone’s streets are broad and well-lit, and its canals are sparkling and clean. Polite conformity is the order of the day, and anything out of the ordinary is met with a twitched curtain and a call to the Bluecoats. Aristocrat mansions of fine marble blockwork and rich timbers sit uneasily next to the cutting-edge architecture of new money. Private autopods are common on the wide roads, and the sky is busy with airships. Behind the high walls and expensive security systems, some of the most debauched hedonism in all of Doskvol takes place.

Landmarks

1 Penderyn Air Terminal. A bold, streamlined tower built some 50 years ago as a mooring station for the growing number of luxury passenger airships. Only the wealthiest can afford tickets. The Ministry of Preservation uses a much lighter touch than at Gaddoc Station, so it’s relatively easy for the rich to bring small amounts of contraband in and out as they please.

2 Silver Market. A curated open-air emporium overlooking the North Hook channel. Silver Market is strictly administrated and very expensive. The latest trends from elsewhere in the Imperium are repackaged and resold here at a significant premium. Avant-garde fashion, luxury autopods, the latest music, rare distillations, and many other vices of the wealthy (to 392 which the City Watch turns a blind eye).

3 The Sanctorium. The chief cathedral of the Church. A towering edifice of buttresses and spires, at least 600 years old. In a courtyard at its heart grows a rowan tree, huge and miraculous with vibrant green leaves.

4 The Dagger Room. The hottest restaurant in Doskvol, serving a modern take on traditional Dagger Isle cuisine. The rich and famous come here to be seen, and the waiting list is months long. Chef Kalani, a young and ambitious culinary genius, changes the menu every day. The walls are decorated with hundreds of blades of every size and shape.

Details

Traits

Scene: Cheerful Bluecoats patrol the streets with a whistle and a smile, moving along any undesirables with swift brutality. Wealthy citizens stroll along the high street, browsing luxury stores.

Streets: Broad, clean, well-lit. Oak Boulevard , Victory Square , Garnet Road , Ivory Lane.

Buildings: Grand stone manor houses with automated security sensors, modern townhouses made of bold architectural forms, opulent theaters and restaurants, luxury shops.

Notables

Wealth ●●●● Security & Safety ●●●● Criminal Influence ●●●● Paranormal Activity ●● ●●

Most engagement rolls suffer -1d due to heavy Bluecoat patrols. Operations against the wealthy in Brightstone are considered on “hostile turf” for the purpose of generating Heat.

Apex Urmston. A high-ranking member of the Church, and its chief officiate for Brightstone. ( Condescending, Zealous, Austere )

Lord Bowmore. While Lady Bowmore is an executive officer in the Ministry of Stability and a terribly powerful woman, Lord Bowmore is an idle aristocrat with little sense and too much time on his hands. ( Bored, Dreamer, Gullible )

Audrey Berrow. Born to humble origins, Berrow is now a hugely wealthy shipping magnate. Famous for the lavish parties she holds at her cantilevered mansion that soars out over the North Hook channel. ( Disarming, Shrewd, Ambitious )

Shady Factions & Ward Bosses

Strangford-Michter Financial (p. 380), Silk (p. 379), the Kethrys Lodge (p. 363)

Charhollow

A crowded residential district home to factory workers, dockers, sailors, laborers, construction workers, and custodians. Charhollow burned down (again) in the Demon Fire of 938, and city planners took the opportunity to replace the crumbling tenements of the previous century with modern concrete housing blocks, functional and utilitarian. A lack of investment and maintenance meant these housing projects soon suffered from the same poor living conditions as the old tenements. Those who could afford it (mainly Akorosi workers) moved to the new district of Radiance, leaving Charhollow with a majority population of Skov, Severosi, Tycherosi, and Daggerites. Considered little more than a dangerous slum by outsiders, the local community is nevertheless close-knit, protective, and proud of where they come from.

Landmarks

1 Danger-Fist Academy. A training hall run by Daxon Danger, an underworld hand-for-hire and expert in five different ancient martial disciplines. Many wayward youths of Charhollow have found purpose and self-worth here.

2 The Program. A community-run cooperative that provides free food and social services to the citizens of Charhollow. The authorities give the Program a hard time, accusing it of being secretly funded by the United Skov Covenant as a front to recruit spies and informants.

Lock Road Books. An eccentric 3 little bookstore that also functions as a kaff house and bar where avantgarde poets recite their latest work. Many of the city’s most prominent intellectuals can be found in a shadowy corner here during an evening’s performance.

4 Strangford House. A large, fenced estate perched on the hill of a private island overlooking Charhollow. The grounds are filled with cuttingedge sculptures. The mansion itself is a modernist marvel of architecture, glass and steel soaring into the air. Raging parties are held here regularly.

Details

Traits

Scene: Laborers returning from work shout greetings to friends and families. The loud rattle and ding of a busy gambling-machine hall. Gang members call challenges from the roof of one tower block to another. Riotcoats (heavily armed and armored Bluecoats) shake down a frightened shopkeeper.

Streets: Narrow alleys, walls of graffiti, flights of dilapidated concrete steps, rusted iron railings. Common Street , Hummock Road , Back Lane.

Wealth ●●●● Security & Safety ● ●●● Criminal Influence ●● ●● Paranormal Activity ●●●●

Operations against the citizenry in Charhollow are considered on “hostile turf ” for the purpose of generating Heat.

Buildings: Huge concrete housing blocks, cheap boarding houses, run-down bars and gambling halls.

Notables

Greene. A private detective who frequently takes on cases from those too poor to pay, offering justice where the Bluecoats won’t. ( Independent, Confident, Fearless ).

Skora. A civil rights leader who agitates for better schools, jobs, and housing in Charhollow. ( Thoughtful, Persuasive, Radical ).

Auntie Chen. A community-minded gang leader who controls four towers, and has banned drug dealing on her turf. ( Assertive, Tough, Just ).

Shady Factions & Ward Bosses

SCORPION Syndicate (p. 376), Red Torque (p. 375), the Burn Corps (p. 351), D-13 (p. 356)

Charterhall

Charterhall houses the corridors of power; the main offices of government including the courts, licensing and taxation offices, records archives, the City Council, and the Imperial Intelligence Agency. The historic buildings, weathered monuments, and dreary streets play host to a hidden world of espionage and deadly games of cat-andmouse. Charterhall is shrouded in an atmosphere of secrecy and betrayal; backroom deals happen in dimly lit pubs, smoky clubs, or quiet meeting places.

Landmarks

Cairn House. The Imperial

Intelligence Agency headquarters in Dosvkol. An unassuming concrete building, overshadowed by the historic grandeur that surrounds it. Most of the Palace’s real work happens in the field; Cairn House itself is a quiet and austere place, where office workers push trolleys of paperwork and senior directors smoke cigarettes in large boardrooms.

Ministry of Discovery Research

Complex. A large facility near Charterhall University consisting of several sleek geometric buildings. The most exciting and experimental visions of tomorrow are explored here. A secret launch facility lies underground, the site of the top secret Blueraker project.

Bellweather Electroplasmic

Fusion Station. When a person dies, their echo in the resonance field remains. Electroplasmic fusion takes 396 this remnant, this resonance echo, and

converts it into raw power. The Fusion Station itself generates the Bubble; a huge energy field that keeps the quantum uncertainty of the deathlands at bay, providing a life of stability and safety for those inside. The station also provides enough power for all the conveniences of modern life, from street lights to plasmovision. Jointly operated by Section 6 and the Ministry of Stability.

The B.L.U.E. Array. The BethanLusk Union Experiment was

a groundbreaking discovery; the scientists modulated the frequency of an electroplasmic fusion field to create a moment of daylight. This led to the B.L.U.E. Array, a striking apparatus of curving steel that towers overhead. It “tunes” the frequency of the Bubble, creating an hour of hyper-real blue skies and daylight (bluetime) above Doskvol at noon every day. The exact nature of “the Blue” is still being investigated.

Details

Scene: A man in a heavy coat passes an unmarked envelope to a woman with one eye. Government workers in dull brown suits hurry to and fro in the rain. Beneath a classical arch, a briefcase changes hands in a seamless exchange. Shadows move behind the closed curtains of a posh townhouse. Two strangers sit on a park bench, exchanging pleasantries loaded with hidden meaning.

Streets: Gray, broad, busy. Imperial Avenue , Seventh Street , Old College Road , Morriston Street.

Buildings: Imposing old stone buildings with columns and classical sculptural motifs. Newer government buildings of Brutalist concrete and glass.

Notables

Traits

Wealth ●●●● Security & Safety ●●●● Criminal Influence ●●●● Paranormal Activity ●●●●

The records in Charterhall can be of particular interest to criminal sorts. Take a Devil’s Bargain for +1d to gather info here, and the GM will tick a clock (the spy agencies watch everyone here, and keep files on anyone who might be useful to them in future).

Doctor Fisher. A leading theoretician at Charterhall University working in the field of pharmacology. ( Brilliant, Humble )

The Milliner. An unassuming hat-maker who works from a small backroom amidst the busy tailors of Charterhall. A well-respected, truly neutral information broker. ( Fastidious, Sharp ).

Professor Akov. A professor of literature at Charterhall University, secretly working for Blackthorn Park. Selects promising students to be recruited as assets. ( Approachable, Reassuring, Perceptive ).

Shady Factions & Ward Bosses

Strangford-Michter Financial (p. 380), the Dirt Barons (p. 358), the Yammies (p. 385)

Coalridge

Coalridge is an economic powerhouse, full of factories and industrial plants that work around the clock. Capital flows freely here; the factories are state-of-theart, automation is everywhere, and there are several well-funded R&D labs and scientific facilities. Even the residential areas are used as test labs, with a dozen pilot programs for various pharmaceutical and technological interventions. Tightly controlled and highly productive, Coalridge is a chilling vision of one potential future for Doskvol.

Landmarks

Coalridge Deep Excavation.

Formerly a coal mine, and the site of the first permanent settlement at the river delta. The ancient history of pre-cataclysm habitation here has left a strong impression in the resonance field, which makes these extensive underground tunnels the perfect place for paranormal testing. Strange experiments are performed, and stranger things discovered.

2 Xantara Pharm Complex. Behind a high chainlink fence, lit by blinding floodlights, this sprawling manufacturing plant churns out pills for the many Xantara Pharm dispensaries and vending machines scattered across the city.

3 Heavy Industrial Zone. The primary hub for steel-making, machine tool building, and chemical 398 processing. Human employees work

alongside hull laborers in vast, brightly lit facilities. Worker efficiency is closely monitored.

New Brickston. Once one of the 4 roughest neighborhoods in the city, before it was torn down and rebuilt as dedicated worker housing. The first testing ground for the Model Worker Initiative, which trials everything from the Lusk Reform Technique to Xantara Pharm’s “productivity enhancing” pill regimen to New Dawn’s wellness program, all under a panopticon-like system of surveillance and control.

Nutriwafer Distribution Hub.

Coalridge was the first district to trial a free allotment of nutriwafer available to any gainfully employed citizen.

Details

Scene: Workers in beige jumpsuits lining up for their nutriwafer. A wellness parable broadcasting from an array of public plasmovision screens. A mechanical red eye scanning the crowd. Industrial monorail cargo being unloaded by crane. Loudspeakers announce a new shift rotation. A Reformed convict, vacant and glassy-eyed, sweeping the street.

Streets: Multi-level, brightly lit, sterile. Metal gantries overhead. Expansion Street , Black Rock Drive , Crane Street , Ambition Street , Enterprise Drive , Hulston Street.

Traits

Wealth ●● ●● Security & Safety ●●● ● Criminal Influence ●●●● Paranormal Activity ●● ●●

Take +1d when you work on long-term projects with Attune in Coalridge Deep Excavation. The GM will tick a clock.

Buildings: Towering conglomerations of capsulesized worker apartments, big boxy factories, industrial plants made of shining steel.

Notables

Hatcher. Head of the private firm that runs security in New Brickston. A true believer in what that project represents. ( Idealistic, Obsessed, Cruel )

Kulozik. Former chief scientist, who has ventured deeper into the Coalridge Excavation than anyone else. His fringe ideas only became more unhinged after his experiences down in the depths. ( Weird, Visionary ).

Shirl. Leader of the Human Hand, an anarchist resistance group in New Brickston. Carries mental scars from its various experimental programs. ( Righteous, Resolute, Haunted ).

Shady Factions & Ward Bosses

Strangford-Michter Financial (p. 380), Xantara Pharm (p. 384), SICU (p. 378)

Crowfoot

Crowfoot is at a crossroads, literally and metaphorically. Sitting at the heart of Doskvol, Crowfoot has traditionally combined many qualities from neighboring districts: the illicit vices of Silkshore, the labor and trade of the Docks, the poverty of Charhollow, and the classic architecture of Charterhall. Recent years have also seen an influx of wealthier residents and businesses, bringing an increase in property values and rents. This displacement of the original population and the loss of community identity has led to many conflicts over resources and territory.

Landmarks

The Warehouse. The hottest 1 nightclub in Crowfoot, with an infamously selective admission policy. To gain entry is a status symbol, and it is rumored people have died trying to sneak in through the vents. The theatrical interior dazzles with glitter and mirrored tiles. Wild parties, celebrity clientele, and abundant party pills.

The Anserekh Museum. A 2 museum of Iruvian artifacts housed in a large old mansion. Dimly lit rooms lined with dusty display cases contain an array of relics from the distant past. Ancient scrolls, ornate swords and daggers, and intricately carved statues.

3 Fort Keel. A ramshackle tower from before the cataclysm that serves as headquarters for the district’s criminal kingpins, the Keel Twins. A warren of lounge bars, gambling dens, fighting rings, and even more illicit activities. Bluecoats won’t dare enter.

4 Apple Lane. A vibrant hub of youth culture and creative expression, with numerous fashion boutiques, record stores, and music clubs lining the narrow street. Not just a commercial destination, but the ultimate place to be seen.

Details

Scene: A nervous aristocrat drives past slowly, looking for a place to get their fix. Fashionable youth strut down the street. Troublemakers abandon a splash of bright graffiti as the Bluecoats come running. Intellectuals sit at a small table debating philosophy over kaff and cigarettes.

Streets: Multi-level and twisting. Neon signs glow through the fog. Ash Way , Gray Street , Magpie Lane , Wicker Alley , Great Cinder Street.

Buildings: Fashion boutiques next door to squalid drug dens and flophouses. A florist operating out of a modular kiosk. A stack of capsule rooms in a new hotel complex.

Traits

Wealth ●●● ● Security & Safety ●● ●● Criminal Influence ●●● ● Paranormal Activity ●● ●●

Anomalies are commonplace in Crowfoot. You may take a Devil’s Bargain for +1d for a daring stunt, but the GM will introduce a new anomaly of time or space to the scene (gravity distortion, temporal hiccup, etc.).

Notables

Captain Stanton. Bluecoat commander. To the wealthy new residents of Crowfoot, Stanton is a cheerful and welcoming presence. To the poorer established communities, he’s the constant boot on their neck. ( Jolly, Short-Tempered, Petty )

Clawson Nether. Property developer and highranking member of the Foundation. Candidate for the Voice of Crowfoot. ( Talkative, Confident, Unrelenting )

Lorna Harby. Modernist artist. Lorna’s family has lived in Crowfoot for generations, and she’s an unofficial spokesperson for the working class families of her housing block. ( Respected, Perceptive, Proud )

Shady Factions & Ward Bosses

The Keel Twins (p. 362), the Limmerfield Gang (p. 365), D-13 (p. 356), the Fighting Goats (p. 359)

The Docks

The docks are a shadow of their former glory; the last leviathans disappeared long ago, and most cargo goes by rail or air these days. The hustle and bustle has been replaced by dark alleys, abandoned warehouses, and rusting ships. Fishing boats still ply their trade, however, and a few eccentrics find other ways to make a living from the Void Sea.

Landmarks

1 The Nightbreaker. One of the last leviathan hunter ships, abandoned at port and left to decay where she was berthed. The rusting carcass is a titanic maze of echoing darkness. Some desperate squatters and criminal gangs inhabit the wreck, and there are rumors of lost treasure deep within the hold.

2 The Void Sea Aquarium and Research Facility. Perched on the edge of the water, the Void Sea Aquarium is a sprawling complex with faded attractions, cracked portholes, and a few shabby submersibles. Dusty display cases house aged fossils and forgotten artifacts, while grimy glass tanks hold murky shapes moving in dark water. Many say the place is a disaster waiting to happen, and that the Void Sea is not something that should be explored or experimented with.

3 Ink Lane. A narrow backstreet, home to some legendary tattoo parlors as well as the city’s tabloids. Loathed by the establishment, these newspapers churn out conspiracy theories, populist rhetoric, and news of the weird and unbelievable. Buried in the sensationalist trash is some of the most provocative and revealing investigation work printed anywhere.

4 Shed 13. By day just another empty warehouse; by night the dimly lit interior is home to the most brutal bare-knuckle boxing ring in the city. The enigmatic proprietor, Klass, takes surprisingly gentle care of those who lose their fight—although some survivors are never seen again after entering his care.

Details

Scene: Seabirds squawking as workers on a trawler mend their nets. Two clandestine figures meeting in the shadows. A street musician playing a melancholy song beneath the soft glow of a lamppost.

Streets: Wide, lonely avenues lined with narrow alleys. Slaughter Row, Crab Street , Ink Lane , Deadwake Street , Fishwrapper Alley.

Buildings: Massive cargo warehouses. Vacant buildings. Dilapidated bars, brothels, and tattoo parlors. Cheap and dangerous bunkhouses.

Traits

Traits
Wealth ●●●●
Security & Safety ●●●●
Criminal Influence ●●●●
Paranormal Activity ●●●●

The many empty buildings and abandoned warehouses make this district a perfect location for a criminal lair.

Notables

Shapcott. An infamous tabloid ink rake. Wanted dead by many powerful figures, both legitimate and criminal. ( Dishevelled, Sharp, Persistent )

Whitegrave. Xantara Pharm has some sensitive and highly confidential operations that are run out of the docks, and Whitegrave ensures these remain secret—at any cost. ( Calm, Precise, Ruthless )

Brine. Some people say Brine is a vigilante, others say Brine is a criminal thug. Some people say Brine was born this way, others say they fell into a vat of byproduct in one of Xantara Pharm’s secret facilities. People say a lot of things, but Brine just lets their fists do the talking. ( Brooding, ShortTempered, Formidable )

Shady Factions & Ward Bosses

The Limmerfield Gang (p. 365), SCORPION Syndicate (p. 376), the Golden Hand (p. 361), Xantara Pharm (p. 384)

Dunslough

For those born into the destitute poverty of the Dunslough Housing Estate, there are two likely fates: incarceration in Ironhook Prison, or signing their life away to the North Hook Military Camp next door. Dunslough is where the iron fist of the state is flexed openly; the citizens are regarded as no more than a raw commodity, to be used up and tossed aside.

Landmarks

1 Ironhook Prison. An antiquated penitentiary that hasn’t changed much in a hundred years (other than accumulating more rust). Conditions are brutal. The most well-connected prisoners manage a comfortable stay, and may even continue to run their criminal enterprises from behind bars.

2 Lusk Reform Clinic. A small but well-funded facility, gleaming white and sterile. Home of the experimental “Lusk Reform Technique.” Subjects emerge as the quiet and pliable Reformed, with their resonance echo stripped away to power a new hull.

3 Dunslough Incident Site. The massive pit here has long been stripped of its precious ores and gems by prison labor, but around a decade ago, a top secret anomaly was discovered deep within the quarry. Tall concrete walls were quickly erected, bristling with security sensors and razor wire, behind which scientists in full protective gear continue to explore the confidential “incident” to this day.

North Hook Military Camp.

Tucked away at the edge of the Bubble is a sprawling reminder that this supposed era of peace is not so peaceful after all. A full military base, including training camp, ordnance depot, and military airship field.

Details

Scene: Soldiers marching up the road in formation. Destitute families scrounging for scraps in the ditches. Airship interceptors on training maneuvers roaring through the air. A sewage barge on its way to the treatment plant.

Streets: Cramped and multi-level in the housing estates—some of concrete but many of dirt, sodden into thick black mud. Broad and straight in the army camp. Military Road , Penance Street , Barrier Road , Grace Lane.

Buildings: Decrepit wooden houses, many abandoned from fire damage or fallen in from age. Newer prefabricated housing, already falling apart too. Military buildings arranged in an orderly grid behind chainlink fences and razor wire.

Traits

Traits
Wealth ●●●●
Security & Safety ●●●●
Criminal Influence ●●●●
Paranormal Activity ●●●● Traits Wealth Security & Safety
Criminal Influence
Paranormal Activity

A lot of military equipment finds its way into the black market here. You can take +2d to acquire an asset of military origin in Dunslough, but also accrue +4 Heat.

Notables

Justine Sweetland. Warden of Ironhook Prison. Her power and influence are rapidly waning as funding is moved to the Lusk Reform Clinic. ( Severe, Determined, Calculating )

Noughton. A peculiar resident of the Dunslough Housing Estate who claims to have traveled here from a hundred years ago. ( Haunted, Vulnerable, Resourceful )

Captain Booker. A hotshot interceptor pilot with high security clearance. Likes to drink. ( Confident, Competitive, Impulsive )

Shady Factions & Ward Bosses

D-13 (p. 356), SCORPION Syndicate (p. 376)

New Horizon

The Lost District was ravaged by plague and then abandoned to the deathlands for centuries. The creation of the Bubble allowed it to be reclaimed, and the district of New Horizon was founded. It is a place of faded grandeur, full of towering stone buildings festooned with gargoyles and grotesques. The district is treated with widespread superstition and distrust, and the only people who live here are those too poor to live anywhere else. Many Skovs have been forced to make the slums of New Horizon their home, either by official policy or economic necessity.

Landmarks

1 New Horizon Precinct. The local Bluecoat HQ, a hulking slab covered in security sensors, metal plating, and weapon emplacements. The Bluecoats fear to walk the streets of New Horizon, and the precinct has the air of being under siege. When they do venture out, they do so in heavy riot armor, ready for violence.

2 The Blue Baths. An ancient bathing and leisure complex. Steaming water is delivered by stone aqueducts to a variety of pools and baths. Although New Horizon is typically shunned, clientele come from all over Doskvol to enjoy these famous healing waters. More specialized leisure pursuits are available to VIP members behind closed doors.

3 Free Haven. A guild hall in centuries past. Now a collective of artists are squatting in the vast and crumbling edifice, where they experiment with radical new ways of living and radical new forms of art.

4 Altar Street Market. A cluttered open-air market specializing in second-hand goods, antique curios, rare books, and other treasures salvaged from the catacombs below New Horizon and the deathlands beyond the Bubble.

Details

Traits

Scene: Pirate radio broadcasting over juryrigged speakers. Hundreds of candles burning behind a stained glass window. A cryptic modern dance performance beguiling a crowd of onlookers. A group of riotcoats arresting a few Skov youths for no apparent crime.

Streets: Multi-level cobblestone streets, ancient bridges, narrow and winding alleys. Great Cathedral Street , Carmine Lane , Bell Tower Road.

Buildings: Towering old buildings covered in towers, flying buttresses, and ornate stonework.

Wealth ●●●● Security & Safety ● ●●● Criminal Influence ●●● ● Paranormal Activity ●●● ●

New Horizon does not have a ward boss yet, so crews based here do not have to pay a tithe. It is a chaotic place, however, so all local trouble rolls take -1d; expect more local trouble than usual.

Notables

Tagwitch. A little scoundrel often found chuckling on rooftops or in alleyways. Wanted by the Bluecoats for defacing property. ( Cryptic, Impish, Proud )

Icepick Klev. An infamous contract killer known for their complete discretion. ( Determined, Loyal, Mysterious )

Halsey. A virtuoso Skov cryptographer and mathematician, in hiding. ( Tired, Wary, Brilliant )

Shady Factions

The Tomorrow Program (p. 382), the Blackjacks (p. 348), Mirror House (p. 370)

Nightmarket

Nightmarket is the glittering, beating heart of commerce in Doskvol. Boulevards hum with autopod traffic, advertisements for the latest fads cover every surface, and crowds pour through the many department stores. Cutting-edge fashion and music is appropriated from more avant-garde districts and packaged for sale here. Nightmarket is the promise of the Age of Consensus made manifest—peace, prosperity, and vibrant consumerism.

Landmarks

1 Dundridges. The oldest and most distinguished of the “big three” department stores in Nightmarket. Several stories tall, with several more stories underground. Houses a diverse range of departments, with many surprising goods available in its obscure depths; the Ancient Artifacts department is particularly infamous.

2 Vreen Autodrome. The largest legal autopod racing stadium in Doskvol, offering bright lights, roaring engines, and spectacular crashes. Hums at all hours with racing vehicles, although it’s the night races that draw crowds of thousands.

Night Queen Crossing. A famous intersection at the heart of

Nighmarket, with the eponymous statue at its center. Huge billboards, neon lights, streams of autopods, and vast crowds of pedestrians make this square one of the busiest spectacles in the city.

4 Hours of Wine and Ash. A smokey underground music lounge named for the early hours of the morning. Harlow Baz runs the bar, a towering woman with a whisper-quiet voice. Harlow is no-nonsense and quick to deal with troublemakers, but endlessly patient with lonely drunks looking for a sympathetic ear.

Details

Traits

Scene: The latest musical advertised on a flashing theatre marquee. A group of well-to-do shoppers bustling down the street, laden with dozens of bags and boxes.

Streets: Wide boulevards busy with traffic. Multi-level walkways and pedestrian bridges. Landscaped parks. High-class subterranean avenues. Chalk Street, Cinnamon Lane , Queen Street , Kethrys Boulevard .

Buildings: Towering department stores. Alleys lined with expensive boutique stores. Narrow but lavish private townhouses.

Wealth ●●● ● Security & Safety ●●● ● Criminal Influence ● ●●● Paranormal Activity ●●●●

The goods in Nightmarket are mostly mass-produced and overpriced, but the retail experience is second to none. When you acquire an asset here, take -1d but also clear 2 Stress .

Notables

Black Arrow. An anonymous masked autopod driver who has never lost a race. Many have tried to kill Black Arrow, both on and off the track. (Mysterious, Brooding, Selfless)

Duckworth. Long-serving concierge at Dundridges, as well as an underworld fence and fixer. Duckworth facilitates discrete deals, providing services that may not be strictly legal. (Polite, Sharp, Experienced)

Cressage. A tailor known for cutting-edge fashion statements, whose clientele includes top celebrities and socialites. (Creative, Bold, Well-Informed)

Shady Factions & Ward Bosses

Strangford-Michter Financial (p. 380), the Lounge Rats (p. 367), Silk (p. 379)

Radiance

As agriculture moved to Bowmore Fields, large swathes of land became available in what was formerly Barrowcleft. This land was acquired by the Foundation, and the utopian district of Radiance was created. It was to be a radical blueprint for a new way of living; symmetrical and standardized, all flowing from the totalitarian genius of a single architect. It consists of a series of cruciform concrete megablocks laid out on a strict grid with multi-lane autopod corridors flowing between open plazas, all built on a vast and inhuman scale.

Landmarks

The Underpass. Most of those

who can afford to live in Radiance work office jobs in Six Towers, and this subterranean autopod bypass was built to facilitate express commuting between the two districts. The project was not without controversy, for it plowed through countless historic layers of Doskvol’s subterranean depths.

Megablock 1. The prototype

model for all the megablocks that followed. Each consists of 60 stories of modern and rational design, radiating light and stability along two spines of “teeth.” Almost 3,000 residents live in this tower alone. There are so many identical apartment units that it’s almost impossible to navigate for outsiders, and some who have wandered lost through its hallways have reported stumbling into strange and disturbing anomalies.

Consensus Plaza. Radiance

features a number of open plazas, intended to serve as gathering spaces to encourage social interaction and a sense of community. In practice, the expansive and unobstructed spaces, fields of concrete and grass with towering skyscrapers all around, induce a sense of vertigo and alienation in most people. Residents and visitors alike shun them as eerie and windswept wastelands.

4 The Radiant Heart. “A genuine geometric arrangement leads to repetition, and repetition yields a standard; the perfect form, the living machine.” The words of the famous architect Moreau. If this whole district were a living body, this location is the heart. It was here that the first earth was turned, and it is here that true believers come to feel the pulse of Radiance.

Details

Scene: Service hulls hanging high above the ground conduct routine maintenance on the exterior of a megablock. Private security guards patrol the meticulously clean streets.

Streets: Wide, straight, and concrete, with soaring concrete flyovers. Very little pedestrian access. The Moreau Autoway , Unity Circle , Zenith Avenue , Ascension Drive , the B6.

Buildings: Skyscraper apartment blocks. Boxy concrete buildings for logistics and services. Everything concrete and glass.

Notables

Traits

Wealth ●●● ● Security & Safety ●●● ● Criminal Influence ●●●● Paranormal Activity ●●● ●

The Underpass allows rapid cross-city transit between Six Towers and Radiance. Be warned that many catacombs and crypts were tunneled through during the construction, and you are advised not to stop or leave your vehicle.

Moreau. The visionary architect who regards himself as having single-handedly birthed Radiance, obsessed with finishing the project. A high-ranking member of the Foundation. (Visionary, Fanatical, Proud)

Megablock 4 Body Corporate. Every megablock votes for the members of a governing Body Corporate. Megablock 4 Body Corporate is infamous for the esoteric rules it enforces within the tower, and its disturbing views on the nature of humanity. (Bureaucratic, Tyrannical, Arcane)

Professor Chorlton. A paranormal expert convinced that the entire district of Radiance is a danger to humanity. (Scholarly, Eccentric, Bewildered)

Shady Factions & Ward Bosses

Mirror House (p. 370), the Foundation, the Ankhayat Consortium (p. 347)

Silkshore

Silkshore is known for high-stakes gambling, fabulous excess, and pure indulgence. A dozen landmark hotels and casinos compete to have the biggest stars, the most spectacular shows, and the most luxurious accommodation. The glamorous neon glow and romantic canals hide a seedier side—underworld factions are heavily invested in the casinos, and they use them as a base for money laundering, smuggling, and violent criminal activities.

Landmarks

The Iruvian Sands. The most

famous of the Silkshore casinos, operated by the Ankhayat Consortium. An elaborate facade of waterfalls and palm trees is dominated by a single towering obelisk of black glass.

Blackstar Hotel & Casino. A vast

glass dome that sparkles with light from a hundred crystal chandeliers within. Each gaming table on the casino floor moves independently on a complex circuit of tracks, creating an orrery that reflects the movements of celestial bodies in the sky.

Spark Graveyard. A former

fairground, long abandoned. Now a resting place for elaborate neon signage of the many failed casinos and hotels that come and go in Silkshore. Forgotten animatronics rot beneath the towering skeletons of weathered metal signs.

The Velvet Veil. An ever-

shifting casino, where countless labyrinthine passages lead to hidden gaming rooms and lounges. There are rumors of secret tables where patrons can wager not only currency but also secrets, memories, and dreams.

5 Fogcrest. A high, peaked hilltop crisscrossed by a maze of narrow stairways and tall rowhouses. Home to the most exclusive designer boutiques and luxury fashion houses, as well as the studios of the most famous of modern artists.

Ankhayat Autodrome. The

second-largest autodrome in Doskvol, situated in the middle of the leafy expanse of Ankhayat Park. Not as large as Vreen’s autodrome, but far more refined. Expect fewer explosive crashes, and more high society.

Details

Scene: Gondolas gliding along canals. Finely dressed guests arrive at a charity gala. A dishevelled gambler being tossed out of a casino by a couple of heavies. The clattering of roulette wheels and the jingle of slot machines. An entertainer dressed in sequins and feathers smokes a quick cigarette down a side alley.

Streets: The streets are interrupted at every turn by a bridge over a tiny canal. Moonwater , Larkwater , Highwater , Taffeta Road , Bridge of Crowns.

Buildings: Traditional buildings with marble columns and intricate frescoes. Towering hotel-casinos adorned with neon lights and elaborate façades.

Traits

Wealth ●●●● Security & Safety ●● ●● Criminal Influence ●●●● Paranormal Activity ● ●●●

Take +1d when you Indulge Vice in Silkshore, but if you overindulge, take level 2 harm (in addition to any other consequences). The underworld heavies that run the district do not treat troublemakers gently.

Notables

Lucky Lucella. A high-rolling gambler with business interests in real estate, finance, and luxury goods. (Suave, Audacious, Self-Absorbed)

Mack Ransom. A notorious casino developer and operator. Leads an extravagant and flamboyant life, punctuated by outbursts of extreme violence. Currently provoking warfare between criminal families so he can scoop up their casinos on the cheap. (Charming, Volatile, Ruthless)

Lady Risbury. Art collector with a passion for rare masterpieces and hosting exclusive exhibitions. (Discerning, Gracious, Esoteric)

Shady Factions & Ward Bosses

The Ankhayat Consortium (p. 347), the Gallo Family (p. 360), the Lounge Rats (p. 367), the Golden Hand (p. 361), the Dirt Barons (p. 358)

Six Towers

A gloomy district, shrouded in seemingly perpetual rain and fog. Six Towers is the business center of the city, dominated by imposing office blocks and skyscrapers where hives of white-collar workers earn their salaries. These new commercial towers are built on haunted foundations; look closer in the narrow alleys and shadowy corners and you’ll still find leering gargoyles, gothic stonework, and an ineffable sense of unease. It’s best not to dwell on all that; commerce never sleeps, and there’s work to be done.

Landmarks

1 Saltfords Central. A raw, Brutalist fist of concrete punching up into the sky, headquarters of the most profitable bank in Doskvol. It’s rumored that there’s a vast gold reserve kept in the depths beneath the building. The security measures are second to none.

2 The Redfog Building. Once an ancient crumbling tower, then turned into a skyscraper decorated with geometric patterns and a dazzling crown of burnished red metal. An iconic part of the Doskvol skyline. A stone aqueduct that still connects the tower to New Horizon has been preserved as a tourist attraction.

3 The New Dawn Center. Home of the popular but esoteric health and wellness cult. The two wings of the building extend out to embrace you from both sides. There are no windows, and the activities within are shrouded in mystery. Visitors welcome.

4 Dimmer House. A humble, squat office building occupying prime real estate. No one is ever seen entering or leaving. The paperwork indicates that it’s owned by “Dimmer Enterprises,” but that’s all anybody knows.

Details

Scene: A group of salary workers, suits and briefcases, hurries through the fog. Autopods speed past spraying rainwater. A crow lands on the traffic light, muttering something incomprehensible.

Streets: Busy thoroughfares cloaked in the shadows of the skyscrapers above. Narrow alleys between the towers. Granite Avenue , Bear Lane , Convent Road , the Executive Parkway .

Buildings: Huge office blocks of concrete and glass. The occasional piece of iron fretwork, stone gargoyle, or marble cornerstone left standing amidst the concrete modernism.

Notables

Traits

|---|---|---|

You may invest up to 4 stacks in the stock market in Six Towers. Invested stacks may only be withdrawn like bank (2 for 1). Each downtime, roll 1d per invested stack. On a 1-3, lose 1 invested stack. On a 4/5, you break even. On a 6, pocket 1 stack as dividends (on a critical, pocket 2 stacks).

Martley Orlov. The creative director at Tottle Orlov Arran, one of the biggest advertising agencies in Doskvol. Where does their genius come from? (Persuasive, Confident, Haunted)

Tyraxis-1. A machine the size of a room. One of the earliest sparkmind prototypes, since made redundant by newer models. Usually found at the end of an alley playing music for small change. (Brilliant, Creative, Complicated)

Prichard. Refuses to sell their decrepit manor to predatory property developers, despite intense pressure. (Weird, Suspicious, Petty)

Shady Factions & Ward Bosses

Strangford-Michter Financial (p. 380), Saltfords Banking Group, Lord Scurlock (p. 366), Mirror House (p. 370), Xantara Pharm (p. 384), New Dawn (p. 371)

Whitecrown

Whitecrown is perched upon the high cliffs of an island, across North Hook channel from the city proper. Sprawling estates contain the stunning mansions of the wealthy; some are traditional marble and stone, while others are the swooping curves of steel and glass that characterize modern architecture. The district is a world unto itself, the home of true power and absolute privilege. Most citizens will never even set foot here.

Landmarks

1 Lair of the Unseen. Some conspiracists claim that Red Queen Island is the secret HQ of the Unseen, a clandestine organization that supposedly controls the powerful like puppets on a string. The urban legend suggests that the dormant volcano contains a state-of-the-art base from which they coordinate their many nefarious schemes. Fanciful, obviously.

2 Premier House. A former military stronghold built on a monumental scale. Now the residence of the Premier, their family, and governmental aides. The Premier is appointed by the Crown as the official representative of central government in the city. They host many galas and banquets, as well as top secret meetings with officials from the military and spy agencies.

Doskvol Academy. Hailed as one

of the finer institutions of learning in the Imperium, where young scions learn to wield various levers of power. A picturesque campus of expansive lawns, tree-lined walkways, and stately brick buildings covered in climbing vines. The Palace aggressively recruits new agents here.

Greenbird Mansion. Greenbird

is a “self-made” millionaire, and to the distress of their old-money neighbors they enjoy nothing more than hosting extravagant social gatherings. The mansion is famous for its avant-garde design and the wild but highly exclusive pool parties held here.

Score Rolodex

Details

Scene: A sleek, luxury autopod is waved through the security gate of a private estate. Workers prepare for an upcoming gala, unloading immense floral displays. Uniformed guards on patrol.

Streets: Broad and well-lit thoroughfares. Regimental Way , Imperial Boulevard , Great Lion Street.

Buildings: Cutting-edge examples of modern architecture; sweeping curves of glass that soar into the sky on cantilevered steel. High balconies, elevated walkways, and private monorails.

Notables

Traits

Wealth ●●●● Security & Safety ●●●● Criminal Influence ●●●● Paranormal Activity ●● ●●

Scores in Whitecrown suffer -2d to the engagement roll and +1 Heat due to state-of-the-art security systems and heavy Bluecoat patrols. However, the payoff will always include +3 stacks compared to a usual score of that magnitude.

The First Warden. Commander of Section 6. Inducts new Reality Wardens at their estate here, as well as undertaking various top secret experimental projects. (Cold, Strategic, Unorthodox)

Silver. The pianist at Paddles, an exclusive private members club. Long-standing informant working for Blackthorn Park. (Discrete, Cautious, Dedicated)

Kenton Sevoy. Celebrity business magnate turned eccentric recluse. Hasn’t left their mansion in years. (Charismatic, Paranoid, Eccentric)

Shady Factions & Ward Bosses

SCORPION Syndicate (p. 376), the Unseen (p. 383), the Tomorrow Program (p. 382)

Score Rolodex

Use these tables to help describe locations. Either roll randomly, or choose whatever grabs you, or just use them as a starting point for your own ideas. Consider mixing and matching score themes to keep things interesting (scientific rooms and gear in a trendy new mansion, or a heavy industrial area in an office building).

Street Obstacles

||||| |---|---|---|---| |1|–|3|4—6| |1|Quarantine/lockdown|Traffic jam| |2|Parade|Construction/demolition| |3|Riot|Gang warfare| |4|Street performers|Paranormal incident| |5|Protest|Bluecoat checkpoint| |6|Street evangelist|Street closed by flooding|

Offices

Areas

  • 1 Reception lobby. Raw concrete walls, high ceiling. Worn carpet tiles, water cooler. 2 Conference room. Long teak table. Ashtray overflowing with cigarette butts.

  • 3 Break room. Vinyl chairs with cracked upholstery. Kaff pot. Indoor plant. 4 File storage room. Smell of dust and paper. Endless steel filing cabinets. 5 Corner office. Large desk. Black rotary phone, ornate cigarette lighter, drinks trolley. 6 Secretarial pool. Dozens of desks and typewriters. Tall stacks of papers.

Also janitor’s closet, elevator hall, restrooms, rooftop area, basement, etc.

Items of Interest

|||| |---|---|---| |1|Rolodex|Leather briefcase| |2|Stack of manila folders|Overhead projector| |3|Intercom|Shredded document in a waste bin| |4|Typewriter with half-typed document|Dictaphone with spools of tape| |5|Fire alarm|Keypunch machine with a stack of cards| |6|Torn memo papers pinned to corkboards|Stack of carbon copy paper|

Obstacles

Impressions

  • 1 Humming fluorescent lights 2 Typewriter clacking 3 Musty paper 4 Stale kaff 5 Static from an office radio 6 Steady ticking from a large wall clock

Industrial Facility

Areas

  • 1 Assembly line. Conveyor belts and heavy machinery.

  • 2 Packing area. Boxes, packing materials, wooden pallets.

  • 3 Storage warehouse. Rows of shelves. Warehouse hulls working round the clock.

  • 4 Control Room. Dials, gauges, large control panels with hundreds of switches and lights. 5 Maintenance Room. Tools, spare parts, metal shelving, the smell of oil and metal. 6 Boiler room. Heavy-duty boiler. A tangle of pipes and exhaust fans. Handwheels.

Also loading dock, waste processing area, coolant area, hydraulics room, R&D workshop, etc.

Items of Interest

|||| |---|---|---| |1|Toolbox|Lubricant canister| |2|Thick, bound instruction manual|Industrial fan| |3|Heavy-duty power cable|Forklift| |4|Blueprints|Fire extinguisher| |5|Coolant pipe|Inventory log| |6|Fuel drum|Precision instruments|

Obstacles

Impressions

Scientific Facility

Areas

  • 1 Main lab. Experimental apparatus, complex machinery, steel workbenches.

  • 2 Testing area. Test dummies, diagnostic instruments, recording devices.

  • 3 Prototype storage. Climate-controlled vaults, with airlock access.

  • 4 Clean room. Sterile, bright, white.

  • 5 Research offices. Typewriters, terminals, filing cabinets. 6 Data processing. Room-sized punch card computer.

Also reception area, chemical storage, radiation shielding room, conference room, observation deck, cryogenics lab, biological research lab, library, etc.

Items of Interest

|||| |---|---|---| |1|Computing terminal|Schematics| |2|Punch card|Data printouts| |3|Microscopes and spectrometers|Calculations on a chalkboard| |4|Cryogenic tank|Incomprehensible, jury-rigged device| |5|Chemical reagents|Scientist’s journal| |6|High-voltage electroplasmic generator|Glassware full of volatile chemicals|

Obstacles

Impressions

Trendy Mansion

Areas

  • 1 Sunken lounge. Low-set with plush seating and bubble chairs. Large windows.

  • 2 Bar area. Wood-paneled walls, shag carpet, crystal decanters.

  • 3 Rooftop terrace. Lounge chairs, lush plants, a stunning view.

  • 4 Extensive gardens. Geometric topiary, modern sculptural art. A large pool, hedge maze.

  • 5 Master bedroom. Mirrored walls, a huge circular bed, velvet drapes. 6 Kitchen. Shining countertops, automated appliances, the latest gadgets.

Also a den, meditation room, dance floor, dressing room, underground garage, panic room, etc.

Items of Interest

|||| |---|---|---| |1|Huge plasmovision set|Key to safety deposit box| |2|Secret doors and passageways|Bold, abstract paintings| |3|Blackmail material|Hidden safe| |4|Rare vinyl records|Mysterious briefcase| |5|High-end autopods|Heavy, expensive rug| |6|Grand piano on a mezzanine|Emergency stash of gold bars|

Obstacles

Impressions

Old Mansion

Areas

  • 1 Grand foyer. A faded red rug, a grand staircase.

  • 2 Library. Tall bookshelves crammed with leather-bound volumes. A secret door.

  • 3 Ballroom. Worn parquet floors, chandeliers, velvet curtains. Smell of floor polish and dust.

  • 4 Servant’s quarters. Narrow corridor with small, modest rooms, many empty and disused.

  • 5 Wine cellar. Cobwebbed bottles and barrels. A locked vault for rare vintages.

  • 6 Portrait gallery. Every inch of every wall covered in stern-faced portraits.

Also kitchens, study, drawing room, attic, dining room, trophy room, primary bedroom, basement laboratory, music room, etc.

Items of Interest

|||| |---|---|---| |Battered leather satchel containing cash| |1|Umbrella stand, with ornate cane-sword| |and a loaded gun| |2|Ornate jewelry box|Porcelain doll| |3|Leather-bound ledger|Weathered travel trunk| |4|Large, sparking fuse box|Portable reel-to-reel tape recorder| |5|Heavy iron lockbox|Heavy chandelier above| |6|Stack of old photographs|Suit of armor|

Obstacles

  • 1 Sudden blackout power failure 2 Resonance entity that has long haunted the place 3 Elaborate, modern security setup—hidden cameras and electrified locks 4 Secret passageways used by enemies 5 Tripwires and antique traps 6 Unexpected guests arrive

4Scratchy music from a phonograph 5 Fireplace crackling 6 Faint smell of mildew

Casino

Areas

  • 1 Main floor. Slot machines, card tables, gambling wheels, many patrons. 2 Showroom. A dazzling stage with sequined singers and dancers. 3 Cocktail lounge. Mood lighting, leather booths, cigar smoke. 4 High-roller suites. Private rooms for VIPs, with the most exclusive tables. 5 Security control room. Surveillance monitors, intercoms, radio sets. 6 Underground counting room. Sterile, brightly lit, highly secure.

Also the grand lobby, private card rooms, a vault, an adjoining hotel, luxury penthouse with private elevator, dance club, restaurant, backroom offices, etc.

Items of Interest

  • 6 High-profile celebrity guest with large security team

Impressions

Apartment Block

Areas

  • 1 Lobby. Tiled floor, wood-paneled walls. Metal mailboxes.

  • 2 Basement. Laundry, boiler room, incinerator.

  • 3 Hallways. Long, narrow. Orange carpet, beige walls.

  • 4 Run-down apartment. Faded, peeling floral wallpaper. A bead curtain. Chipped green tiles.

  • 5 Tidy apartment. Brown carpet, mustard-yellow couch. Plastic, glass side table. Strange apartment. Purple velvet curtains (closed). Shelves of dusty brass figurines and

  • 6 porcelain ornaments, some with occult significance. A bed bolted to the ceiling above you.

Also a stairwell, elevator, roof terrace, communal lounge, maintenance room, fire escape.

Items of Interest

|||| |---|---|---| |1|Cryptic message left on the bulletin board|Rent payment in an envelope| |2|Utility closet (supplies, wiring board)|Abandoned basement storage unit| |3|Fire escape access|Elevator maintenance panel| |4|Laundry chute|Old photo album in a false-bottom drawer| |5|Detailed tenant files|Hidden diary| |6|Reel of security camera footage|Janitor’s ring of building keys|

Obstacles

Impressions

Anomalous Zone

Areas

  • Gravity well. Furniture floats in mid-air, or has dropped upside-down to the ceiling.

  • 1 Gravity may shift randomly and violently.

  • Shifting passage. Hallways and rooms that rearrange themselves constantly, creating

  • 2 new pathways or sealing off exits.

  • The void. A vast space of bright white light or complete darkness. Voices and other noises

  • 3 seem to come from everywhere and nowhere.

  • Temporal glitch. Past, present, and future bleed into one another, revealing scenes from

  • 4 different times in overlapping layers.

  • Hall of mirrors. The mirrors show parallel dimensions, with alternate versions of the

  • 5 viewer—realities that are softer or harsher, doppelgangers that have had different life experiences, that pursued different professions, that are kinder, or crueler. Memory spheres. A space filled with floating orbs of light. Touching an orb revisits a

  • 6 crucial memory—your own, or someone else’s.

Also a room overgrown with dense plant life, a room of infinite recursive staircases, a room filled with a fire that doesn’t burn, a room that contains a raging snowstorm, a doorway that leads to a woodland forest.

Obstacles

Impressions

General Descriptions

Building Style

  • 1 Modernist. Lots of glass, steel, and concrete. Simple geometric forms. Clean lines.

  • 2 Brutalist. Raw, exposed materials (especially concrete). Heavy, monolithic structures.

  • 3 Old relic. Brickwork or stonework. Ornate detailing and gargoyles. Faded curtains.

  • 4 Corporate chic. Linoleum floors, wood-paneled offices, drop ceilings. Abstract art.

  • 5 Alternative. Mismatched furniture, exotic rugs, painted murals, a haze of incense.

  • 6 Futurist. Rounded forms that soar on cantilevers. Glass domes and bubbles.

Physical Threats

Many factions will have gangs of thugs and goons that may cross paths with the PCs, or they may employ more esoteric forms of physical protection.

  • What’s their look ? Do they wear a particular kind of clothing, like lounge suits or jumpsuits? Does the faction have a common color scheme? Perhaps S CORPION Syndicate’s goons are recognizable by their powder-blue jumpsuits.

  • What tactics do they favor? Do they rely on their speed, stealth, strength, intimidation, high-tech equipment, cunning planning, or clever traps?

Social Threats

Many targets will be insulated from social intrusion by a layer of social threats— servants, bodyguards, assistants, lackeys, or even just bystanders who are in the way. These characters may be a physical threat as well as a social threat. Are they weaker against one approach or the other?

1 – 3 

4—6
  • 1 Meddling sycophant. Inserts themself where they aren’t needed to gain the favor of a higher power. Offers unwanted help, then informs on every action and conversation to their superior. Constantly hovers around, drawing unwanted attention.

Scheming, ambitious underling. Always looking to advance their position, either through their own success or by kneecapping those ahead of them in the hierarchy. Self-serving power plays.

  • 2 Paranoid bureaucrat. Scrutinizes every detail and document. Insists on overly complicated procedures, intrusive questions, and excessive paperwork.

Bored, oblivious dilettante. Easily manipulated. Reckless and indifferent to consequences. Overlooks important information, has no attention to detail.

  • 3 Bumbling fool. Well-meaning and friendly, but disastrously incompetent. Knocks over equipment, trips alarms, misunderstands instructions, attracts attention at the worst times, all with a large amount of misplaced confidence.

Nosy gossip. Eavesdrops and asks prying questions. Eager to trade rumors, whether true or not. Talks to everyone, leaks everything.

  • 4 Loyal, suspicious underling. At the bottom of the hierarchy but extremely diligent and dedicated. Sharp instincts, hard to distract, won’t stop following you.

Paranormalist. Psychic abilities pose a unique threat to social approaches. They can sense your thoughts and intentions (do you want to resist that?). Remote viewing, emotional manipulation, telepathic communication, premonitions.

  • 5 Flirtatious manipulator. Uses charm to sway the situation for their own gain. Switches loyalties easily, always seeking their own advantage. Capable of betrayal on a dime, for better or worse.

Cynical opportunist. A self-serving survivor. Deliberately stirs up conflicts or tensions for their own gain. Makes cold calculations and takes ruthless action. Uses other people as means to an end, sees everything as a transaction.

  • 6 Anxious, overeager underling. Desperate to prove their worth. Too helpful—makes rash decisions, shows up when they’re least needed, acts prematurely on plans or instructions. Excessive enthusiasm, and easily panicked.

Petty tyrant. Has a small amount of authority and exercises it to the hilt. Enforces trivial rules and makes vindictive demands. Overreacts to insignificant issues, imposes harsh penalties, and micromanages everything. Expects flattery and servile adulation.
